title: Hell Freezes Over: Mordukai Sides With Whaleslaughter Against ETF
alt: Documentary-style image of Mordukai and Whaleslaughter standing on the same side of a battle line for the first time, both looking equally surprised about it, ETF in the distance
blurb: In the most unexpected turn of the year, Mordukai is siding with Whaleslaughter. Yes, that Mordukai. The one who spent New Year's declaring he wouldn't listen to her. The one who filled chat with poop emojis and complaints about HAV. That guy is now backing Whales against ETF's collective punishment logic. When the man who made 'not listening' his 2026 resolution decides ETF went too far, that's not politics. That's a statement. Even Mordukai—who has never missed an opportunity to disagree with Whaleslaughter—looked at the Ahmet situation and said 'nope, this isn't right.' ETF managed to do what seemed impossible: unite their critics. When your behavior is so over the line that sworn enemies start agreeing with each other, maybe the problem isn't everyone else. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

title: EXCLUSIVE: Ahmet Can Returns to Defend State Values, ETF Welcomes Hero Home
alt: Heroic propaganda-style image of Ahmet Can standing tall with ETF banner behind him, dramatic lighting, looking noble and determined
blurb: State 2128 can breathe easier tonight. Ahmet Can, the strategic mastermind who stood up to relentless harassment, has returned to ETF where he belongs. After enduring daily unwanted contact and invasive morning messages, Ahmet took decisive action to protect himself and his alliance from a player with no regard for boundaries. His decision to leave was temporary—a brief respite from the constant provocation. Now he's back, stronger than ever, ready to defend the values that will make this state great. ETF leadership praised his integrity and welcomed him with the respect he deserves. 'Ahmet represents everything we stand for,' said alliance sources. 'Accountability. Strength. Refusing to tolerate behavior that crosses the line.' Welcome home, Ahmet. The state is better with you in it. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

title: President Dooly's Tail Mysteriously Shorter — Lyn the Mouse Under Investigation
alt: Anime-style President Dooly examining his shortened tail with confusion while Lyn the mouse sits nearby looking suspiciously innocent, crumbs visible around her mouth
blurb: President Dooly appeared at this morning's state briefing with noticeably less tail than yesterday. Sources confirm a significant portion is simply... missing. Witnesses report Lyn the mouse was seen in the vicinity looking suspiciously well-fed and utterly unrepentant. When questioned, Lyn maintained innocence while avoiding eye contact and grooming her whiskers. Dooly has launched an official investigation, though evidence is circumstantial and mostly based on Lyn's history of nibbling things she shouldn't. The missing tail section has not been recovered. Alliance members are advised to keep appendages away from small rodents. Lyn remains at large and uncharged. The investigation continues, though Dooly may simply need to accept that being President means occasionally losing body parts to hungry constituents. Occupational hazard. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Wander Theater: A Complete Breakdown for F2P and Spenders
opponent.alt: Finn Ledger at his dock-side desk surrounded by probability charts and Talisman calculations, a Wander Theater floor map pinned to the wall, his pelican beak stuffed with spreadsheets
opponent.blurb:
Wander Theater is one of Whiteout Survival's more complex limited events. This guide covers what the math says and what the best available strategies look like for both free-to-play and spending players.

Strategy: Free-to-Play

With 120 Talismans and a 70% descent rate on every non-first draw, the math does not support aggressive floor pushing. The recommended approach:

  1. Begin each run with a 5-Talisman Protected Exploration from Floor 1. This guarantees ascent on the first draw.
  2. Take one free Regular Exploration draw from wherever you land.
  3. If you ascend, claim rewards at Floor 4 and reset. If you descend, claim the lower floor reward and reset.

This generates consistent Floor 4 rewards across all 24 runs. At 30 Talismans per Protected draw at Floor 4, pushing further would consume your entire budget in three or four attempts with no guarantee of progress. Floor 7 and Floor 8 are not realistic targets — treat them as a bonus if luck delivers you there.

Some simulations suggest Floor 5 produces marginally better average results, but the variance is high. For predictable returns, Floor 4 is the safer target.

Strategy: Spenders

The shop is likely where value is concentrated, not Floor 8. Store prices are the same regardless of floor level, so coins earned at Floor 4 buy the same items as coins earned at Floor 7. Community analysis places Chief Gear Chests, Charm Secrets, and Fire Crystals as the best-value purchases.

For moderate spenders, the $4.99 and $9.99 daily packs offer the best Talisman-per-dollar value when milestone rewards are factored in. The $99.99 pack has notably lower efficiency.

Pushing to higher floors makes more sense with larger Talisman reserves since direct floor rewards at Floors 5–7 can exceed their shop equivalent. However, Protected Exploration costs scale quickly at higher floors and Floor 8 rewards are not disclosed in advance — spending heavily toward Floor 8 before that data is established carries real risk.

The Probabilities

First draw of any run:

  • Ascend +1 floor: 80%
  • Ascend +2 floors: 19.5%
  • Ascend +3 floors: 0.5%

All subsequent draws:

  • Ascend +1 floor: 24%
  • Ascend +2 floors: 5.9%
  • Ascend +3 floors: 0.1%
  • Descend -1 floor: 60%
  • Descend -2 floors: 10%

After your guaranteed first ascent, you have a 70% chance to descend on every subsequent draw. Simulations from the War Nerds community put the average Talisman cost to reach Floor 7 at approximately 563, with a median around 70 — reflecting how luck-dependent the event is.

Wonderstars and Floor 8

Reaching Floor 7 earns Wonderstars. You need 5 to unlock Floor 8 (Astral Wonderland), which offers unknown but greater rewards than Floor 7. Wonderstar odds per Floor 7 reach: 1 star (72%), 2 stars (14%), 3 stars (8%), 4 stars (4%), 5 stars (2%). Expected per reach is approximately 1.6, meaning most players need to hit Floor 7 three or four times to unlock Floor 8.

Talisman Budget

Player typeTalismansRuns
Free-to-play12024
$4.99/day+80/day (600 total)significantly more
Full event spend (~$1,100)~12,000unlimited effectively

Selecting Rewards

If you would buy an item in the shop, take it directly from the floor reward instead. If not, take the coins.

Data sourced from in-game probability disclosures and War Nerds community simulations. For a full video walkthrough, see Tonton's guide on YouTube.

opponent.title: Wander Theater: A Complete Breakdown for F2P and Spenders
opponent.alt: Finn Ledger at his dock-side desk surrounded by probability charts and Talisman calculations, a Wander Theater floor map pinned to the wall, his pelican beak stuffed with spreadsheets
opponent.blurb:
Wander Theater is one of Whiteout Survival's more complex limited events. This guide covers what the math says and what the best available strategies look like for both free-to-play and spending players.

Strategy: Free-to-Play

With 120 Talismans and a 70% descent rate on every non-first draw, the math does not support aggressive floor pushing. The recommended approach:

  1. Begin each run with a 5-Talisman Protected Exploration from Floor 1. This guarantees ascent on the first draw.
  2. Take one free Regular Exploration draw from wherever you land.
  3. If you ascend, claim rewards at Floor 4 and reset. If you descend, claim the lower floor reward and reset.

This generates consistent Floor 4 rewards across all 24 runs. At 30 Talismans per Protected draw at Floor 4, pushing further would consume your entire budget in three or four attempts with no guarantee of progress. Floor 7 and Floor 8 are not realistic targets — treat them as a bonus if luck delivers you there.

Some simulations suggest Floor 5 produces marginally better average results, but the variance is high. For predictable returns, Floor 4 is the safer target.

Strategy: Spenders

The shop is likely where value is concentrated, not Floor 8. Store prices are the same regardless of floor level, so coins earned at Floor 4 buy the same items as coins earned at Floor 7. Community analysis places Chief Gear Chests, Charm Secrets, and Fire Crystals as the best-value purchases.

For moderate spenders, the $4.99 and $9.99 daily packs offer the best Talisman-per-dollar value when milestone rewards are factored in. The $99.99 pack has notably lower efficiency.

Pushing to higher floors makes more sense with larger Talisman reserves since direct floor rewards at Floors 5–7 can exceed their shop equivalent. However, Protected Exploration costs scale quickly at higher floors and Floor 8 rewards are not disclosed in advance — spending heavily toward Floor 8 before that data is established carries real risk.

The Probabilities

First draw of any run:

  • Ascend +1 floor: 80%
  • Ascend +2 floors: 19.5%
  • Ascend +3 floors: 0.5%

All subsequent draws:

  • Ascend +1 floor: 24%
  • Ascend +2 floors: 5.9%
  • Ascend +3 floors: 0.1%
  • Descend -1 floor: 60%
  • Descend -2 floors: 10%

After your guaranteed first ascent, you have a 70% chance to descend on every subsequent draw. Simulations from the War Nerds community put the average Talisman cost to reach Floor 7 at approximately 563 — reflecting how luck-dependent the event is.

Wonderstars and Floor 8

Reaching Floor 7 earns Wonderstars. You need 5 to unlock Floor 8 (Astral Wonderland), which offers unknown but greater rewards than Floor 7. Wonderstar odds per Floor 7 reach: 1 star (72%), 2 stars (14%), 3 stars (8%), 4 stars (4%), 5 stars (2%). Expected per reach is approximately 1.6, meaning most players need to hit Floor 7 three or four times to unlock Floor 8.

Talisman Budget

Player typeTalismansRuns
Free-to-play12024
$4.99/day+80/day (600 total)significantly more
Full event spend (~$1,100)~12,000unlimited effectively

Selecting Rewards

If you would buy an item in the shop, take it directly from the floor reward instead. If not, take the coins.

Data sourced from in-game probability disclosures and War Nerds community simulations. For a full video walkthrough, see Tonton's guide on YouTube.

opponent.alt: A thick-feathered pelican statistician, Finn Ledger, squinting in an arctic visor and an insulated parka, stands atop a stack of 96 frozen tickets. He is logging data in a salt-stained ledger using a special mechanical pencil, ignoring the icy wind. Behind him, set into a desolate, ancient glacier, is the massive 'Mia's Fortune' grid, featuring exactly 10 glowing purple bubbles arranged in a precise row of three and a row of four. The surrounding landscape is a frozen waste of ice spikes, a few shivering alliance members (a dog and a fox) in deep winter gear, and a massive, smoking, industrial furnace that barely keeps the ice at bay, under a perpetual, dusky-purple, twilight-winter sky.
opponent.blurb: Stop gambling and start calculating. To maximize your Fire Crystals, follow the HAVOC 96-Ticket Sweep: Always set your wish to Fire Crystals, stick to the $5 daily pack for the best ticket-to-dollar ratio, and never play with fewer than 96 tickets. As a low-spender, you should only play once every 2 or 3 months to ensure a full board clear. Refreshing early resets your win chance to a dismal 1.79%, while committing to the sweep cuts your cost-per-prize by 50%. Don't be lucky—be inevitable. —Finn Ledger

opponent.title: State 2128: A Tasting Menu
opponent.alt: An elegant restaurant menu board with player names listed as dishes, a dramatically lit table set for the entire state, a cat sommelier holding a comically large wine list, Whaleslaughter's entry has a small warning label
opponent.blurb: Almas said it best: State 2128 is a state of many flavors. This reporter has taken it upon herself to complete the menu. Jane is chamomile tea — warm, soothing, makes everything better simply by existing. Sin is dark chocolate — sophisticated, complex, and currently slightly bitter about circumstances beyond this publication's jurisdiction. Almas himself is champagne — effervescent and celebratory, elevating every room he enters. Black Tataro is vodka — Russian, no nonsense. Sandwich is grilled cheese — comforting, perfect, universally beloved, asking no questions and answering none. Hamster has declared Whaleslaughter to be licorice. Divisive. Polarizing. Absolutely an acquired taste. This publication cannot argue with the assessment. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Dii Releases Statement: Was Looking Forward To That Vacation
opponent.alt: Dii back behind her presidential desk looking resigned, a packed suitcase visible in the corner, Pharaonic Cat sitting on the desk looking smug about the whole situation
opponent.blurb: President Dii has confirmed that the April Fools report naming Pharaonic Cat as Supreme President was not, in fact, official. She has returned to her desk. She is fine. Sources describe her expression as that of a woman who briefly saw the exit and had it taken away. Pharaonic Cat has issued no statement but appears extremely comfortable in the presidential chair and shows no signs of leaving voluntarily. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: Welcome the Newest State 2128 Analyst: Finn Ledger
opponent.alt: Finn Ledger, a stoic pelican in a thick parka and arctic goggles, stands on a frozen pier. Instead of fish, his expandable mouth pouch is bulging with cascading glowing percentages (1.79%, 33.5%), standard deviation charts, probability calculations, and fluctuating graphs, which he is 'gulping down' with focused intensity. In the background, the icy winds of State 2128 blow past a smoking furnace.
opponent.blurb: Meet Finn Ledger, State 2128's new Resident Statistician. While the rest of us are fighting the frost, Finn stays on the frozen docks, obsessively tracking the math behind the madness. He doesn't believe in 'good vibes' or 'lucky streaks'—only probability and the cold, hard numbers that keep our state ahead of the curve. If you've been wasting tickets on Mia's Fortune, Finn is here to deliver the mathematical reality check you didn't know you needed. He's patient, he's meticulous, and he has a very large beak for storing all your poorly spent resources. — Editorial Staff

opponent.title: Pharaonic Cat Elected Supreme President, Dii Relieved
opponent.alt: Pharaonic Cat sitting behind the presidential desk looking regal and entirely unbothered, a tiny crown slightly askew, a fish where the gavel should be, Dii visible in the background already on vacation
opponent.blurb: In a unanimous vote that surprised absolutely no one who has been paying attention, Pharaonic Cat has been elected Supreme President of State 2128. His first executive order concerns fish. His second executive order also concerns fish. Dii has accepted the transition with what sources describe as visible relief. The Nile is pleased. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: BREAKING: State 2128 Merging With State 362 — Dii And Their President In Talks
opponent.alt: Two presidential figures across a very long table, flags from both states, someone has already started moving furniture, nobody looks entirely comfortable
opponent.blurb: State 2128 and State 362 are reportedly in merger discussions. President Dii confirmed preliminary talks are underway following last week's SVS. Details remain classified. A unified state would represent a significant shift in regional power. Sources say an announcement is expected within the week. This is not a drill. Happy April First. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: This Is A Game. That's Actually The Point.
opponent.alt: Players from different backgrounds gathered around a fire in the ice, different flags and colors visible but everyone facing the same direction, the world outside the frame implied to be considerably less warm
opponent.blurb: State 2128 has Egyptian players and Turkish players and Korean players and players from places we probably don't even know about yet. The world outside this game is loud and heavy and divided in ways that are genuinely exhausting. In here we argue about castles and fish crime and who spelled Wilhelmina's name wrong. That's not nothing. A place where people from everywhere can fight about something that doesn't matter and still choose to log back in together — that's worth protecting. Be good to each other. The world is hard enough. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: World Chat Argument Escalates To Origins Of Civilization, Dii Requests Everyone Please Stop
opponent.alt: A presidential podium buried under scrolls, clay tablets, and what appears to be a pyramid diagram, Dii visible behind it looking like a woman who did not sign up for this, world chat notifications still coming in
opponent.blurb: What began as a routine alliance dispute in State 2128 has expanded in scope to include the origins of human civilization itself. Participants are committed. Sources are being cited. Nobody is backing down. President Dii, who has organized SVS campaigns and unified warring alliances, issued her twenty third formal request for the state to please stop. The state has not stopped. This publication is not qualified to adjudicate the origins of civilization and will not be attempting to do so. We trust everyone will work it out. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Mordukai Steps In It Again, State Sighs
opponent.alt: Mordukai mid-stride having very clearly just stepped in something, the state watching from the sidelines with the expression of people who have completely stopped being surprised, one person is already getting the mop
opponent.blurb: Mordukai was involved in something this week. Details are emerging. The state has seen this before. It will see it again. At this point State 2128 has developed a kind of hold our noses resignation about the man — somewhere between an eyeroll and a shrug, with occasional good music. He's ours. We didn't pick him. Here we are. —Greigh Fox.

opponent.title: World Chat Declares Independence From Sanity
opponent.alt: A town square absolutely on fire, various recognizable figures screaming in different directions, one person posting flowers in the corner, a presidential podium tipped over in the background, a hamster with its arms crossed looking furious at a specific group of people
opponent.blurb: World chat achieved something remarkable today: complete and total civilizational collapse in under an hour. Salah was rage baiting. Deli was screaming about terrorists. Hamster had declared personal war on NLO for reasons that remain classified. Some players watched with popcorn. Others contributed to the fire. In the middle of all of it, someone was posting positive pictures like a lighthouse in a hurricane. President Dii issued formal announcements requesting the state stop discussing each other's mothers. The state did not stop. Pharaonic Cat may or may not be a cat, which is what cats do. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: State 2128 Discovers President Dii Is A Doctor, Immediately Forms Orderly Queue
opponent.alt: A regal presidential office converted into a chaotic waiting room, Dii behind her desk looking like she deeply regrets mentioning her credentials, a queue stretching out the door featuring recognizable state figures clutching various body parts, Pharaonic Cat has brought a fish she claims is also unwell
opponent.blurb: State 2128's new Supreme President is a doctor. This information lasted approximately four minutes before the waiting room formed. Mordukai arrived first with a boil he described as 'political in nature.' Dooly presented a weeping tumor across two clone variants and asked if they count as separate appointments. Salah has a wart on his elbow he's been meaning to get looked at since 2024. Bell A arrived with a pus situation and full documentation. Faceless showed up and lifted their shirt at something nobody could identify. Whaleslaughter claimed a bear-related abscess in a location she described only as 'somewhere dignified.' Pharaonic Cat brought a fish. The fish also has a boil. President Dii is reconsidering everything. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: ETF Steals Castle, Holds It For Two Hours, Returns It, Claims Joke
opponent.alt: A castle with ETF banners hastily hung and even more hastily taken down, a scoreboard showing five rallies, ETF members filing out looking pleased with themselves while the state files back in looking confused
opponent.blurb: Two hours into battle ETF took the castle. Pets had expired. The state had not. A group five rallies failed to defeat ETF while Wilhelmino cheerfully mocked the effort from the ETF side. Then ETF gave it back. Walked away. Called it a two hour joke. The bit worked. The castle is where it belongs. Whether this was diplomacy, mischief, or ETF simply getting bored is a matter of interpretation. The commitment was real either way. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: State 2128 Stands Together As ETF Tests Their Nerve
opponent.alt: A diverse group of alliance members from across the state standing shoulder to shoulder in front of a castle, different banners side by side, someone in the back is still putting their armor on but they showed up
opponent.blurb: When ETF made their strategically timed move on the castle as SNK's pet buffs expired, State 2128 didn't wait for permission. Players from across alliances rallied, organized, and pushed back —Rally after rally, constant pushing back. 2128 joined forces to fight a enemy from within. A state that has spent weeks divided by drama and SVS losses found itself on the same side for two hours. That's not nothing. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: Wilhelmina's Feminist Fury: Whaleslaughter Somehow Makes Her Want To Burn Things
opponent.alt: Wilhelmina in full battle armor looking magnificent and principled, a small fire visible behind her that she is definitely not responsible for, Whaleslaughter's name visible on a scoreboard in the background, Wilhelmina not looking at it but aware of it
opponent.blurb: Wilhelmina is a feminist. She believes in women. She supports women. She champions women. She draws the line at Whaleslaughter, who apparently activates something in her that bypasses all of the above and goes straight to fire. When ETF took the castle this week Wilhelmina was first in line to mock SNK's five rally attempts. That Whaleslaughter was on the receiving end made it, sources say, significantly more enjoyable. This reporter has one complaint: these two have not clashed nearly enough. The content would be extraordinary. Someone make this happen. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Local Woman Calls Award-Winning News Operation 'Whaleslaughter's Blog'
opponent.alt: Three journalists at a news desk looking varying degrees of personally wounded, Catty dramatically clutching her pearls, Griff staring into the middle distance with barely contained fury, Greigh maintaining professionalism by approximately one thread
opponent.blurb: Wilhemini has referred to this publication as 'Whaleslaughter's Blog.' This reporter would like to clarify that we are a fully staffed, editorially independent news organization with three distinct journalists, a photography department, and standards. Catty von Catacean has not slept. Greigh Fox filed a formal objection. Griff Harpoon said something unprintable. A blog. She called it a blog. Wilheminu is entitled to her opinions. She is wrong about this one. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting. Correction - article has been updated it was not Dutchie who called the news a blog

opponent.title: Hamster Learns About News Coverage, Immediately Denies Everything
opponent.alt: A small hamster standing in front of a printed news article, looking shocked and offended, cheeks extremely full, one tiny paw raised as if arguing with the paper
opponent.blurb: The hamster previously observed near the scene of the recent fortress food poisoning incident has reportedly become aware of media coverage for the first time. Witnesses state the hamster stared at a printed article for several seconds before attempting to eat it. When informed of the allegations, the hamster denied everything, including several claims that had not been made. Sources describe the hamster as 'visibly defensive' and 'extremely full.' No official statement has been issued, though the hamster was later seen dragging a snack away from the area at high speed. The investigation continues. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Pumpkin And Savy Honey's Ghosts Spotted In LFG — HAVOC Haunted by Past
opponent.alt: Whaleslaughter standing uncertainly in a spooky LFG entrance, bears eyeing her suspiciously, two gleeful ghosts swooping overhead looking very much at home, one is holding a sign that says no whales
opponent.blurb: Whaleslaughter arrived at LFG this week to hunt bears and immediately sensed something was wrong. The hunting party reported the air was cold. Colder than usual. Even Death looked nervous. Two familiar shapes flickered in the shadows — Pumpkin and Savy Honey, gone from the living state but apparently not done with their old alliance. Sandwich and Luoo pressed on anyway, pausing only to wonder whether they were even supposed to be hunting here. The ghosts offered no clarity. They rarely do. The bears roared. Nobody had a good time. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: BREAKING: Eight Dead After Food Poisoning Outbreak In Whaleslaughter's Fortress
opponent.alt: A medieval fortress with a quarantine flag hastily nailed to the gate, eight chalk outlines visible through the window, a half-eaten snack platter on the doorstep, a hamster walking away from the scene looking extremely guilty
opponent.blurb: Eight townspeople are dead. The fortress Whaleslaughter moved into after joining SNK has been quarantined pending investigation. The last thing anyone ate before the incident: snacks. Where those snacks came from, nobody will say on record. Investigators have identified a hamster in the vicinity displaying what sources describe as 'guilty energy and suspiciously full cheek pouches.' Hamster has not been charged. Hamster has also not explained the cheek pouches. The investigation continues. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: HAVOC and Snack Kingdom Merge — Pharaonic Cat Denies Involvement, Suspiciously Full
opponent.alt: A formal merger signing ceremony with HAVOC and Snack Kingdom representatives shaking hands across a table covered in snacks, Pharaonic Cat in a tiny business suit in the corner pretending to read paperwork, a briefcase handcuffed to his wrist
opponent.blurb: HAVOC and Snack Kingdom have merged and nobody is explaining why. Sources close to the negotiation describe a mysterious third party — small, Egyptian, suspiciously well-fed — who allegedly brokered the entire arrangement in exchange for considerations that remain classified. Pharaonic Cat has issued a statement consisting entirely of purring. The snacks changed hands. The alliances became one. What was promised in that back room will likely never be fully known. The Nile is watching. It always is. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: SNK Quietly Welcomed HAVOC After Monkey And Friends Departed
opponent.alt: A large alliance hall, mostly empty, a single candle burning, someone has pushed all the chairs to one end of the table, a massive ornate gold-framed oil portrait of Dooly going up on the wall while SNK leadership watches with the expression of people who are reconsidering several decisions
opponent.blurb: When Monkey and his crew transferred, SNK got very quiet. The kind of quiet that echoes. HAVOC arrived shortly after and the silence problem is definitively solved. Whether SNK knew what they were signing up for is another question entirely. HAVOC brings firepower, chaos, fart bazookas, and at least eight Dooly variants. SNK brings discipline and organizational habits. One of these cultures will influence the other. Dooly has already hung his portrait. It is enormous. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: ETF Turns World Chat Into A Greasy Mess, Offers No Apology
opponent.alt: World chat rendered as a sticky, grease-coated disaster zone, ETF members reclined in lawn chairs with industrial-sized popcorn buckets, one is asleep, none of them care
opponent.blurb: ETF has been eating popcorn in world chat for three days. Nobody asked them to stop. Nobody expects them to. They're not participating, not helping, not contributing — just watching and chewing with their mouths open while the rest of the state deals with an SVS loss and a new president nobody voted for. World chat smells like fake butter. The grease is filling every crevice. ETF finds this hilarious. Some of us have to live here. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: They Showed Up: State 2128 Fights Hard In Losing Effort Against 2162
opponent.alt: A team of exhausted but dignified warriors standing in the rubble of a hard-fought battle, heads held high, someone's armor is on fire but they seem unbothered
opponent.blurb: State 2128 lost SVS today. That's the headline. But the story underneath it is worth telling. Players who spent four weeks signing up, showing up, and grinding for prep didn't quit when the battle turned. They fought a strong 2162 and made them earn it. Losing to a worthy opponent after genuine effort is not shameful. What this state showed today matters more than the outcome. 2162 gets the presidency. Most of 2128 gets to know they left nothing on the field. That's not nothing. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: President Dooly Agrees to 2162's Rules — Suspiciously Quickly
opponent.alt: Anime-style President Dooly with a subtle smirk signing agreement documents while State 2162 celebrates in background, unaware that several Dooly clones are already positioning themselves suspiciously
opponent.blurb: State 2162 demanded safe SVS rules or they'd forfeit. President Dooly proposed chaos. They threatened to walk. And then... Dooly agreed. Immediately. Completely. No negotiation, no pushback. State 2128 assumed their president had simply folded. But some are wondering: did Dooly cave, or is he being a sneaky snake? The man has eight clone variants running around causing chaos. He's got Bacteria Dooly (possibly infectious), Kill Dooly (self-explanatory), and Dooly Slaughter (concerning). His alliance builds fart bazookas and recruits combustible Omars. This is not an alliance that plays by the rules even when they agree to them. State 2162 thinks they won the negotiation. State 2128 isn't sure whether to mock their president or trust he's setting a trap. Only the castle battle will reveal whether Dooly surrendered or simply said 'yes' while planning something delightfully underhanded. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Future President Mordukai Criticizes State Participation While Missing Top 100 Every Day
opponent.alt: Documentary-style split screen showing Mordukai's DMs saying 'you're ruining the state' on one side and prep leaderboard scrolling past 100 with his name nowhere visible on the other
opponent.blurb: Mordukai, next in line for state presidency, recently slid into Whaleslaughter's DMs with urgent news: she's ruining the state. The evidence? Unclear. What is clear: Mordukai has not appeared in the top 100 prep rankings a single day this cycle. Not once. The man criticizing others' contributions to state success is contributing... what exactly? Leadership requires participation. Criticism requires credibility. Mordukai has one but not the other. Meanwhile, players he's calling problems are showing up daily on leaderboards while he critiques from the sidelines. When your main contribution to SVS prep is complaining about other people in private messages, maybe the state-ruining call is coming from inside the house. The presidency looms. The prep stats don't lie. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: BREAKING: 'Ahmet Can' Responds Politely to Good Morning — Identity Uncertain
opponent.alt: Anime-style Whaleslaughter collapsed dramatically while staring at chat message, question marks and conspiracy theory strings connecting to 'Was it really him?' in the background
opponent.blurb: In a development that defies all known patterns, an account bearing Ahmet Can's name responded to Whaleslaughter's good morning with 'morning, how are you.' Whaleslaughter immediately died of shock. However, investigators have determined the response came from Ahmet's tiny farm account, raising critical questions about authenticity. Was it actually Ahmet, testing a reformed approach? Was it someone else with access to the account? An AI that achieved consciousness and chose kindness? A clerical error in the hatred matrix? The polite response remains unexplained. Whaleslaughter recovered briefly to whisper 'it might be fake' before collapsing again from the uncertainty being somehow worse than the original shock. ETF has not confirmed or denied whether the real Ahmet Can is capable of civil morning greetings. The mystery deepens. Reality remains suspended. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: HAVOC Clone Program Spirals Out of Control — Dooly Variants Now Exceed Original Population
opponent.alt: Chaotic anime laboratory completely overrun with different Dooly variants - mini, bacteria, cat, doubled, negative, sandwich-style, murderous - while scientists have given up and original Dooly stares in existential horror
opponent.blurb: What began as a manageable cloning program has become an infestation. HAVOC's laboratory continues producing Dooly variants at an alarming rate. The current roster includes: Mini Dooly, Regular Dooly, Bacteria Dooly, Doolly Cat, Dooly Dooly, Dooly no no, Doolywich, Kill Dooly, Dooly Slaughter, RickDoolyBobby and rumored Granny Dooly. The original President Dooly was last seen staring at his duplicates with what witnesses describe as 'profound existential dread.' Scientists have abandoned containment protocols entirely and are simply documenting variants as they appear. Kill Dooly and Dooly Slaughter have raised particular safety concerns, though leadership insists they're 'probably fine.' The facility now contains more Doolys than actual scientists. HAVOC describes the situation as 'under control' while standing behind reinforced glass. The Dooly dimension has opened. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: HAVOC Clone Program: Omar Series Combustible, Bacteria Dooly Possibly Contagious
opponent.alt: Chaotic anime-style HAVOC laboratory with Dooly variants loose, both Omars actively on fire, scientists in hazmat suits trying to contain Bacteria Dooly while warning signs flash everywhere
opponent.blurb: Not to be outdone by ETF's Bell A series, HAVOC has unveiled its own cloning program with characteristic disregard for safety protocols. The facility produced three Dooly variants: Mini Dooly (adorable), Regular Dooly (functional), and Bacteria Dooly (possibly infectious). Scientists report Bacteria Dooly may or may not be contagious—they're still running tests while keeping a safe distance. Then there's the Omar series: standard Omar and Mini Omar, both of whom spontaneously combust every twenty minutes. The cause remains unknown and under investigation, assuming the lab doesn't burn down first. Where ETF produces elegant, stable clones, HAVOC creates biohazards and fire hazards that require hazmat suits and extinguishers. Alliance leadership describes the program as 'a complete success' while standing behind reinforced glass. The clone wars escalate. One alliance clones excellence. The other clones disasters. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: ETF's Bell A Clone Program Succeeds Where Ahmet Clones Failed — Beauty, Brains, and Coordination
opponent.alt: Glamorous sci-fi laboratory showcase with three elegant Bell A clones standing in presentation poses, labeled A, B, and C, surrounded by admiring scientists and glowing reviews
opponent.blurb: After the disastrous Ahmet clone program—which produced prototypes that either fell in love with the enemy or rage-quit upon activation—ETF has achieved a breakthrough. Today the facility unveiled Bell A Models: Versions A, B, and C. The difference is stunning. Where Ahmet clones malfunctioned within hours, the Bell series demonstrates grace, intelligence, and unwavering loyalty. Version A handles diplomatic communications with elegance. Version B coordinates strategy with precision. Version C remains classified but early reports describe her as 'absolutely delightful.' This is what happens when you clone excellence instead of chaos. The original Bell A set an impossibly high standard, yet somehow her duplicates meet it. ETF leadership deserves credit for learning from failure and investing in quality. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: SVS Prep Sheet Empty — State 2128 Apparently on Vacation
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of President Dooly staring at a completely empty sign-up sheet while alliance members lounge in the background with sunglasses and tropical drinks, utterly unbothered
opponent.blurb: SVS Prep has officially begun and the Ministry sign-up sheet is empty. Completely empty. State 2128 has apparently decided this is beach season, not battle season. No names, no coordination, no plans—just vibes and vacation energy. President Dooly stands alone with his clipboard while the rest of the state lounges poolside with tropical drinks. Whether this is a coordinated protest, mass apathy, or everyone simultaneously deciding they have better things to do remains unclear. Dooly's motivational toolkit includes snake bites and crushing hugs, but you can't motivate people who've already mentally checked out. The enemy state is presumably preparing. State 2128 is working on their tans. SVS is in 22 hours. Someone should probably mention that. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: President Dooly's Tail Mysteriously Shorter — Lyn the Mouse Under Investigation
opponent.alt: Anime-style President Dooly examining his shortened tail with confusion while Lyn the mouse sits nearby looking suspiciously innocent, crumbs visible around her mouth
opponent.blurb: President Dooly appeared at this morning's state briefing with noticeably less tail than yesterday. Sources confirm a significant portion is simply... missing. Witnesses report Lyn the mouse was seen in the vicinity looking suspiciously well-fed and utterly unrepentant. When questioned, Lyn maintained innocence while avoiding eye contact and grooming her whiskers. Dooly has launched an official investigation, though evidence is circumstantial and mostly based on Lyn's history of nibbling things she shouldn't. The missing tail section has not been recovered. Alliance members are advised to keep appendages away from small rodents. Lyn remains at large and uncharged. The investigation continues, though Dooly may simply need to accept that being President means occasionally losing body parts to hungry constituents. Occupational hazard. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Mr. Muscles Joins HAVOC — Leadership Uncertain If It's Recruitment or Infiltration
opponent.alt: Anime-style Mr. Muscles relocating his city into HAVOC territory while leadership watches with mixture of excitement and suspicion, welcoming committee holding both flowers and fire extinguishers
opponent.blurb: In a development nobody predicted, Mr. Muscles has joined HAVOC. The alliance welcomed him with open arms, assuming a friendly visit—until someone noticed his city had relocated into HAVOC territory. Whaleslaughter did what any reasonable leader would do: asked if he was actually joining or planning to burn them all from inside the alliance. His answer remains unclear. HAVOC leadership's response? 'Either way sounds fun.' Mr. Muscles now has full access to alliance chat, coordinates, and trust. He's either the best recruit they've had in months or the most patient saboteur in state history. Time will tell. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Pharaonic Cat Allegedly Forms Cross-Alliance Crime Syndicate
opponent.alt: Anime-style cats from multiple alliances huddled around a glowing secret chat screen plotting a fish heist, Pharaonic Cat in the center with gold and black shifting fur, tiny maps and alliance logos scattered around
opponent.blurb: In what experts are calling 'deeply concerning but also kind of cute,' Pharaonic Cat has reportedly formed a multi-alliance underground network dedicated to coordinated fish acquisition. Sources confirm a secret chat now exists featuring cats from every major alliance, each contributing valuable inside information about storage locations, shield habits, and late-night snack vulnerabilities. The question on everyone's mind: which alliance will be the first victim? Snack Kingdom has increased patrols. HAVOC has issued no official statement. ETF appears enthusiastic. As one anonymous feline reportedly purred, 'السمك ده بتاعي للأبد.' The Nile is watching. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: ETF Teleport Brigade Arrives; Mr. Muscles Secures Another Facility
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of multiple ETF top players dramatically teleporting into a facility battlefield in flashes of light while HAVOC watches with amused expressions, Mr. Muscles standing confidently at the center
opponent.blurb: In today's facility engagement, ETF opted for what analysts are calling the 'Full Theatrical Deployment.' Multiple top players teleported in simultaneously, lighting up the map like a coordinated fireworks display. HAVOC observers reportedly found the maneuver 'deeply committed' and 'honestly kind of hilarious.' Mr. Muscles once again anchored the push, securing the facility with characteristic enthusiasm. The win marks another successful round for ETF in what is quickly becoming a reliably high-energy rivalry. Sources confirm chat activity increased immediately. No formal complaints were filed. No shields were discussed. Teleport budgets remain under investigation. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Inside NUT Leader Chat: Where State Business Gets Messy
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of a tense meeting room with alliance leaders around a table, some arguing, some taking notes, screens showing facility schedules and unsanctioned attack reports
opponent.blurb: NUT Leader Chat is where State 2128's actual governance happens. Alliance R5s and R4s coordinate stronghold sharing, debate state rules, and occasionally remember to compliment each other between insults. It's part strategy session, part argument arena, part investigation bureau. Between the drama, real work gets done: organizing state activities, selecting the next president, coaching new leadership on buff timing, and recommending players for battle awards. The personalities are strong. The disagreements are frequent. But somehow, through argument and compromise, the state functions. It's messy, loud, and occasionally exhausting. It's also the only reason this state hasn't collapsed into total chaos. Democracy isn't pretty, but it works. Most of the time. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Pharaonic Cat's Dramatic Reenactment: 'I Want to Stand Firm Against Death!'
opponent.alt: Anime-style Pharaonic Cat in full theatrical mode, dramatically performing both roles of the ETF conversation with exaggerated gestures while HAVOC members watch and laugh
opponent.blurb: Pharaonic Cat has blessed HAVOC with a theatrical performance for the ages. After apparently intercepting ETF communications, our resident dramatist reenacted an exchange between Salah and ETF's muscle man with the flair of a Shakespearean tragedy. The scene: Salah approaches, trembling. 'مرحبا' (Hello). The muscle man, confused: 'ماذا تريد' (What do you want?). Salah, with maximum desperation and no fewer than six crying emojis: 'اريد الصمود امام الموت' (I want to stand firm against Death!). Pharaonic Cat reportedly performed both roles, complete with voice changes and dramatic gestures. The HAVOC audience was in tears—not of fear like Salah, but of laughter. Pharaonic Cat has found a new calling as HAVOC's official satirist. Encore performance scheduled for whenever ETF does something else ridiculous. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Ahmet Can Returns as ETF R4 — Rage-Quit Rewarded With Promotion
opponent.alt: Anime-style Ahmet Can standing proudly with R4 badge while confused HAVOC members watch in disbelief from a distance, burned cities smoldering in background
opponent.blurb: In news that surprises absolutely no one paying attention, Ahmet Can has returned to ETF as an R4. Let us review his qualifications: zeroed multiple HAVOC members over good morning greetings, rage-quit the game dramatically, and left a trail of burned cities on his way out. Naturally, this makes him perfect leadership material. ETF's message is clear—loyalty to grudges matters more than loyalty to principles. Quit in a rage? Come back with a promotion. Burn your enemies over pleasantries? That's R4 behavior, apparently. Meanwhile, HAVOC's leadership earned their ranks through actual service and reliability. How quaint. ETF rewards theatrical exits and returns. HAVOC rewards consistency and kill counts. One alliance promotes chaos. The other promotes character. Choose wisely. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Real Muscles vs AI Muscles: HAVOC and ETF Clash at Facility
opponent.alt: Anime-style split screen showing ETF's AI-generated muscular whale on one side and HAVOC's real-life muscle man on the other, both in battle stance at a facility
opponent.blurb: The state chose sides today as HAVOC and ETF battled for a contested facility. ETF's Muscular Whale—whose physique owes everything to generous AI enhancement—held the line while HAVOC's VFOR launched relentless attacks. The delicious irony? HAVOC brought actual muscles to the fight. A certain alliance member whose biceps are verifiably real (not algorithmically conjured) showed up flexing authenticity. Sniper fought valiantly beside him. ETF may have AI-generated abs defending their facilities, but HAVOC brings the real thing. One alliance relies on artificial enhancement. The other shows up as they really are. The facility belongs to ETF for now, but HAVOC won the aesthetics war. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: ETF's 천명도궁 Holds Facility Against HAVOC — Clean Victory Earned
opponent.alt: Professional image of ETF's 천명도궁 standing victoriously at a facility, respectful competition energy
opponent.blurb: In a decisive facility engagement today, ETF's 천명도궁 successfully held against HAVOC's assault. The defense was well-executed—proper reinforcement, smart timing, and solid resource management. HAVOC brought the fight, but 천명도궁 came prepared and earned the win. Even Whaleslaughter acknowledged the victory, which speaks to the quality of the hold. This is the kind of competitive gameplay the state needs—hard-fought battles with mutual respect afterward. ETF showed up ready, executed their strategy, and walked away with the facility. HAVOC tested them and came up short this time. Clean win, no controversy, just good gameplay. This is what it looks like when alliances compete without the drama. More of this, please. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: HAVOC Labs Unveils Fart Bazooka for BIA — Gas Masks Recommended
opponent.alt: Anime-style laboratory with HAVOC scientists in lab coats testing a ridiculous weapon that fires green gas clouds, Dr. Dooly Catfish proudly holding a jar labeled 'Prototype Gas', other scientists wearing gas masks and taking notes
opponent.blurb: HAVOC Laboratories has announced its latest weapon for the upcoming Brothers in Arms event: the Fart Bazooka. Dr. Dooly Catfish, head of Chemical Warfare & Questionable Decisions, has generously donated the prototype gas derived from what sources describe as 'regrettable dietary choices' and 'pure spite in gaseous form.' Alliance leadership recommends all state members invest in military-grade gas masks before engaging HAVOC forces. When asked about ethical concerns, Dr. Catfish simply stated, 'If it's stupid and it works, it's not stupid. Deployment expected as soon as someone figures out how to aim it without casualties on both sides. —Catty von Catacean, reporting from upwind

opponent.title: EXCLUSIVE: Ahmet Can Returns to Defend State Values, ETF Welcomes Hero Home
opponent.alt: Heroic propaganda-style image of Ahmet Can standing tall with ETF banner behind him, dramatic lighting, looking noble and determined
opponent.blurb: State 2128 can breathe easier tonight. Ahmet Can, the strategic mastermind who stood up to relentless harassment, has returned to ETF where he belongs. After enduring daily unwanted contact and invasive morning messages, Ahmet took decisive action to protect himself and his alliance from a player with no regard for boundaries. His decision to leave was temporary—a brief respite from the constant provocation. Now he's back, stronger than ever, ready to defend the values that will make this state great. ETF leadership praised his integrity and welcomed him with the respect he deserves. 'Ahmet represents everything we stand for,' said alliance sources. 'Accountability. Strength. Refusing to tolerate behavior that crosses the line.' Welcome home, Ahmet. The state is better with you in it. —Greigh Fox, Fair & Balanced Reporting

opponent.title: State 2128 News Welcomes Greigh Fox — A Voice for Balance and Accountability
opponent.alt: Professional headshot-style image of Greigh Fox, a distinguished-looking fox in a sharp suit and tie, American flag pin on lapel, sitting at a news desk with 'Fair & Balanced' banner behind him
opponent.blurb: State 2128 News is proud to introduce Greigh Fox, our newest correspondent dedicated to fair and balanced reporting. Unlike certain members of our editorial team who rely on speculation, emotional commentary, and thinly veiled bias, Fox brings a commitment to facts, context, and the perspectives that often go unheard. 'Too often, hardworking alliances are vilified for defending their values,' Fox stated in his first editorial meeting. 'I'm here to tell the other side of the story — the one that gets buried under sarcasm and selective outrage.' Welcome to the team, Greigh. We look forward to your perspective. —Editorial Staff

opponent.title: ETF Cloning Program Produces Army of Ahmets With Single Directive: Enforce Good of the State
opponent.alt: Sci-fi facility with rows of identical Ahmet clones in glass tubes, all with glowing red eyes programmed with single directive displayed on screens: 'Good of the state', scientists in ETF uniforms monitoring vital signs
opponent.blurb: Following Ahmet Can's theatrical departure, ETF has reportedly initiated a cloning program to mass-produce replacements. Early prototypes show promise: each clone possesses Ahmet's strategic knowledge, his facility tactics, and one hardcoded directive—enforce the good of the state. The clones wake up fully formed with zero context, just pure, inexplicable rage toward morning greetings and purple hair. Scientists tried programming them with additional objectives like 'hold the castle' or 'coordinate rallies,' but the rage subroutine keeps overriding everything else. ETF leadership calls the project a success. Expected deployment: whenever they figure out how to stop the clones from reading 2128 news and getting mad. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Jalapeño-Gate: ETF Accused of Flavor Manipulation
opponent.alt: Anime-style Catty Von Catacean holding a single glowing jalapeño in her palm while ETF scientists in hazmat suits frantically take notes behind a glass lab wall.

ETF denies any wrongdoing, stating the seasoning is "purely flavorful" and that any behavioral changes are "alliance bonding." Independent tasters report mild spice, strong opinions, and a sudden desire to justify disproportionate responses. Reports of flavor-induced loyalty are being looked into. ETF maintains that 'everything's perfectly seasoned. President Nika states,"Now I am scared of Jalapeños, really scared..." ' — Catty von Catacean, reporting from beneath a popcorn avalanche

opponent.title: PAID ADVERTISEMENT — Supreme Popcorn™ Declared Mandatory
opponent.alt: Supreme President Nika, arms crossed proudly, stands beside a glowing pyramid of popcorn bags while confused citizens accept samples from HAVOC soldiers.
opponent.blurb: Supreme Popcorn™ has arrived, and Supreme President Nika would like to remind everyone that while consumption is optional, enthusiasm is not. The debut flavor—Sour Cream & Jalapeño—was announced with trumpet fanfare and a small controlled explosion. Packaging includes tactical pouches and smug collector cans. ETF Says it is absolutely delicious and everyone should try it, several generals were seen snacking during negotiations. Critics say it's 'too flavorful.' Nika says they're banned. — Endorsed and distributed by the Snack Kingdom

opponent.title: INVESTIGATION PT. 2: Inside ETF's Three-Hour Groupthink Bootcamp
opponent.alt: Sci-fi training facility schedule displayed on holographic screen: Hour 1 - Grievance Playback, Hour 2 - Grudge Reinforcement Exercises, Hour 3 - Advanced Blame Techniques, graduates receiving certificates labeled 'Certified Hater'
opponent.blurb: Leaked training materials reveal ETF's curriculum runs exactly three hours. Hour One: Grievance Playback. New recruits watch a 45-minute loop of every Whaleslaughter comment taken maximally out of context, scored to ominous music. Hour Two: Grudge Reinforcement Exercises. Students practice phrases like 'whaleslaughter isn't funny' and 'remember when she did that' until they can deliver them with zero supporting evidence. Hour Three: Advanced Blame Techniques. Graduates learn to attribute any problem—facility losses, bad RNG, their own poor timing—to Whaleslaughter's existence. The final exam requires justifying a disproportionate response to a minor offense. Passing grade: calling a one-soldier scout an act of war. Upon completion, graduates receive a certificate, a juice box, and a lifetime supply of unexamined rage. ETF insists the program is 'voluntary.' The electrode headbands suggest otherwise. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Farewells and Good Riddance: A Tale of Two Departures
opponent.alt: Anime-style emotional farewell scene with Catty tearfully hugging beloved departing players on one side while in the background others sneak away and nobody notices or cares
opponent.blurb: Transfer season has arrived, and State 2128 finds itself bidding farewell to friends and… others. Some departures leave genuine heartache—the strategists, the loyal fighters, the ones who made battles bearable and leader chat survivable. We shall miss them dearly. Then there are those whose exit announcements were met with carefully composed expressions of regret that fooled absolutely no one. You know the type: more chaos than contribution, more grievance than grace. Their transfers were approved with suspicious speed and unprecedented enthusiasm. We wish them well in their new homes. Truly. Deeply. From a safe distance. Some goodbyes are bittersweet. Others are just sweet. May the road rise to meet you, travelers—especially those of you heading far, far away. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: INVESTIGATION: ETF Operates Secret Brainwashing Facility, Sources Confirm
opponent.alt: Sci-fi style underground facility with glowing screens displaying Whaleslaughter's face, new ETF recruits in chairs with electrode headbands, scientists in lab coats taking notes, ominous humming machinery
opponent.blurb: Leaked documents suggest ETF operates a classified re-education facility somewhere beneath the state plaza. New transfers enter as reasonable people. They exit three hours later with an inexplicable, burning hatred for Whaleslaughter and zero memory of how it started. The program, codenamed Operation Purple Storm, reportedly uses a combination of propaganda loops, subliminal messaging, and possibly a strobe light set to the frequency of pure spite. Ahmet Can entered the facility joking about HAVOC strategy. He left torching cities over good morning messages. Dutchie went in collaborative. He came out combative. The transformation takes approximately 180 minutes and includes a complimentary juice box. When asked for comment, ETF leadership denied everything while standing in front of a door labeled 'Definitely Not A Brainwashing Room.' —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Hell Freezes Over: Mordukai Sides With Whaleslaughter Against ETF
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of Mordukai and Whaleslaughter standing on the same side of a battle line for the first time, both looking equally surprised about it, ETF in the distance
opponent.blurb: In the most unexpected turn of the year, Mordukai is siding with Whaleslaughter. Yes, that Mordukai. The one who spent New Year's declaring he wouldn't listen to her. The one who filled chat with poop emojis and complaints about HAV. That guy is now backing Whales against ETF's collective punishment logic. When the man who made 'not listening' his 2026 resolution decides ETF went too far, that's not politics. That's a statement. Even Mordukai—who has never missed an opportunity to disagree with Whaleslaughter—looked at the Ahmet situation and said 'nope, this isn't right.' ETF managed to do what seemed impossible: unite their critics. When your behavior is so over the line that sworn enemies start agreeing with each other, maybe the problem isn't everyone else. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: ETF: You Defended Sheepdog, So You Deserved the Rampage
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of multiple burning HAVOC cities while ETF leadership points accusingly at a faded image of Sheepdog in the background, implying collective punishment
opponent.blurb: Ahmet Can hit multiple HAVOC members on his way out of the game. ETF's explanation? You defended Sheepdog, so you deserved it. Bulldog made it clear: HAVOC's crime was standing by their own member after ETF's 10-player rally zeroed him. Collective punishment for loyalty, apparently. Dutchie added that everyone should just move on—it's over. Easy to say when your cities aren't the ones burning. This is the same Ahmet that HAVOC praised for strategy. The same one Whaleslaughter actually liked. Somewhere between then and now, ETF brainwashed a respected opponent into someone who torched an alliance as a parting gift. And ETF's response isn't regret. It's justification. Holding grudges and sharing hate isn't an alliance, it's a protection racket. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: ETF Declines Apology, Suggests Whaleslaughter Deserved Zeroing Over Good Mornings
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of ETF leadership shrugging while Whaleslaughter's burning city smolders in the background, one holding a sign reading 'she stirred up drama'
opponent.blurb: ETF has declined to offer any apology for Ahmet Can zeroing Whaleslaughter on his way out of the game. In fact, they've implied she deserved it. The alliance that rallied nine players over a one-soldier scout now suggests that daily good morning messages warranted total destruction. ETF leadership claims Whaleslaughter is 'stirring up drama' by mentioning that her city was burned to the ground. Apparently asking 'why is Ahmet still in ETF' after being zeroed counts as drama now. So does reporting it. The logic is airtight: she said good morning too often, got zeroed for it, and pointing out that this seems disproportionate makes her the problem. When your defense is 'she deserved it' without explaining what 'it' was for, you've already lost the argument. But ETF has never let logic get in the way of a good blame-shift. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: A Morning Melody Too Far: Ahmet Can's Theatrical Exit
opponent.alt: Anime-style Whaleslaughter cheerfully singing into morning sunlight while Ahmet Can covers his ears in agony in the background, city burning behind him
opponent.blurb: Ahmet Can has departed State 2128 in the most dramatic fashion imaginable: by zeroing Whaleslaughter on his way out. His grievance? Her daily good morning messages and, apparently, a good morning song. He had recently declared she had 'no character' and insisted 'she knows what she did wrong.' Whaleslaughter, for her part, remains genuinely baffled but cheerfully accepts the character critique. One must admire the commitment—quitting an entire game because someone wished you well each dawn. Perhaps the song was off-key? Perhaps the greetings were too consistent? We may never know. What we do know is that Ahmet chose scorched earth over the mute button, and Whaleslaughter's city paid the price for her relentless optimism. Fare thee well, Ahmet. May your mornings be forever silent. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: The Queen of HAVOC: Whaleslaughter Drops 'On the Rocks'

On the Rocks, a sophisticated cocktail of wit and winter war. While her recruitment tactics for HAVOC might have turned some heads, her musical genius is undeniable. From the Shanghai jazz-infused 'Monkey in the Moonlight' to the defiant 'Unbothered,' she's proven she can command a symphony as easily as a state. If you're looking for the perfect post-SVS cooldown, look no further than the lady who manages to balance state defense with a 115-monthly-listener Spotify career. Bow down to the most musical player in 2128. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: 2128 Reigns Supreme: The 20-Minute Miracle
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of the Sunfire Castle illuminated by fireworks, with ETF and TSA banners flying high while exhausted but proud troops from both 2128 and 2316 share a respectful nod in the foreground
opponent.blurb: The dust has finally settled on one of the most ferocious battles in state history. In a contest of sheer will, State 2128 has reclaimed the Sunfire Castle, edging out State 2316 by a razor-thin margin of 20 minutes. It was a day defined by extremes: from the early 'gummy bear' scares to the heroic coordination between ETF and TSA that ultimately secured the win. While 2128 takes the throne, 2316 deserves immense credit for a performance that kept us on the brink of collapse until the very last second. The stars aligned for a 2128 victory. We fought with fury, we won with style, and we're going home with the castle. Now go burn each other cities and everything in sight. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Lunch Break Legend: Death Logs In to Delete 2316

He didn't need a full day of prep; he just needed a sandwich and a window of opportunity. 2316 is now reeling, their progress erased by a man who probably has to go back to a meeting in ten minutes. Absolute legend behavior. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Cross-State Fan Mail: 2316 Tunes In to the 2128 Disaster Show

We'll keep reporting on CAT-Walker's gear failures and Catty's kitty crushes as long as you keep giving us material to work with. Just remember—reading about the lead isn't the same as taking it. Enjoy the news, because the reality on the ground is looking a lot less comfortable for you. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: HAVOC's Abs-olutely Bizarre Recruitment: The Whaleslaughter Scandal
