Whaleslaughter, maybe put some shirts on the troops—it's getting weird out here. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: The Shade of Victory: Salah Anchors the 2128 Comeback
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of Salah standing atop the Sunfire Castle ramparts, wearing tactical sunglasses and a smirk, while ETF and TSA banners fly side-by-side behind him
opponent.blurb: State 2316 just hit a brick wall, and that wall is wearing designer shades. In a stunning display of unity, ETF and TSA have synchronized their watches and their rallies, successfully reclaiming the lead and planting Salah/조덕배 firmly in the center of the objective. The vibe has shifted from 'desperate' to 'dominant' in a matter of minutes. Salah looks so relaxed in his sunglasses you'd think he was at a beach resort, not the most contested coordinates in the region. This is the 2128 we know—coordinated, fearless, and stylish as hell. If the 2316 'fans' are still reading our news, take a good look at those shades; they're the last thing you're going to see before we close this out. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: The ETF Resurrection: From Gummy Bears to Grizzly Bears
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a massive ETF rally slamming into the castle gates with renewed energy, sparks flying and pet auras glowing bright gold
opponent.blurb: Don't count the kings out just yet. After a brief and embarrassing nap, ETF has roared back to life with a vengeance that has State 2316 checking their exits. The battle for the Sunfire Castle has turned into a literal tug-of-war, with the lead swinging back and forth like a pendulum. Our rally leaders have clearly found some emergency reserves, and the coordination is finally clicking. It's no longer a walkover—it's a war of attrition. If they can maintain this white-knuckle pressure, 2128 might just pull off the comeback of the year. ETF isn't just fighting for the castle; they're fighting to erase the memory of that 10-minute slip. Buckle up, it's going to be a photo finish. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: A Tale of Two Tabbies: Catty's Feline Identity Crisis
opponent.alt: Anime-style landscape of Catty von Catacean standing between two pedestals—one featuring the orange tabby Petrarca and the other featuring the luxurious Persian V FOR, looking utterly torn
opponent.blurb: Oh, the agony of choice! Just as I was preparing my nursing kit for a trip to the 2316 camp to visit the dashing Petrarca, I caught a glimpse of V FOR in the SNK ranks. A Persian! Such volume! Such poise! My heart is currently a battlefield more intense than the Sunfire Castle. Do I stay true to our local 2128 aristocrat, or do I follow the siren call of the ginger rebel from 2316? Griff says I should focus on the 'tactical regrouping,' but how can I talk about troop movements when there is so much majestic fur to admire? Perhaps a joint grooming session is the peace treaty this state truly needs. —Catty von Catacean. Griff's Note: "I'm officially putting in for a transfer to a state where the reporters aren't distracted by dander. V FOR is a top-tier contributor, Catty, not a fashion accessory. Let's focus on the fact that we're currently homeless!"

opponent.title: The Empty Vault: Marwen Tunisia's Heroic Bankruptcy
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a TSA commander standing in a vast, empty warehouse with only a few cobwebs left, looking at a balance sheet of zero gems while smoke rises from the castle in the distance
opponent.blurb: If you want to see the true cost of war, look no further than Marwen Tunisia. The TSA stalwart has officially hit the 'SVS Zero'—no speedups, no resources, no healing kits, and not a single gem left to his name. In his own words, everything he spends months gathering is incinerated in the heat of battle. It's the ultimate sacrifice: a player who gives everything until there's literally nothing left to click. While the big whales worry about their pet juice, players like Marwen are the ones grinding the gears of the state until they smoke. He's going into the post-match phase with empty pockets but a clear conscience. Someone buy this man a resource pack; he's earned it. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: A Nurse for the Vanquished: Catty's Heart Crosses the Line
opponent.alt: Anime-style landscape of Catty von Catacean in a vintage nurse's outfit, holding a first-aid kit while looking wistfully toward the 2316 camp where the orange tabby Petrarca sits regally on a throne of debris
opponent.blurb: I simply must applaud State 2316. Such spirit! Such resilience! And such... well, such Petrarca. While my heart beats for 2128 to reclaim its throne, I find myself deeply moved by the way the CAT alliance has fought to stay in this dance. Petrarca, that magnificent ginger muse, has inspired a certain tenderness in my reporting today. Once the dust settles and the victory is decided (for us, naturally!), I might just find myself wandering over to the 2316 camp with some soothing ointments and a warm blanket. Every brave soldier deserves a gentle touch after such a spirited battle, and Petrarca... well, he deserves a kingdom of his own. Don't worry, darlings, my loyalty remains, but a nurse's work is never done. Wink wink. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Gummy Bear Rallies: The Juice-less Collapse of 2128
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a battle pet looking sad and deflated like a giant gummy bear, while State 2316 marches triumphantly into the Sunfire Castle in the background
opponent.blurb: We didn't just lose the castle; we lost it because our frontline had the offensive power of a bag of gummy bears. ETF's rally leaders apparently forgot that a battle pet is only as good as its last juice box. The moment those buffs expired, our legendary beasts went from city-crushing monsters to sleepy house cats. State 2316 didn't need a miracle; they just needed to wait for our timers to blink out. That 20-minute lead didn't just slip—it plummeted the second our power bars hit red. Now we're sitting on the sidelines, watching 2316 enjoy the view from the throne while our commanders scramble to find their 'refresh' buttons. It's a hard lesson in maintenance: if you don't feed the beast, the beast won't fight. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: A Ginger Gaze: Catty Finds Love in a Hopeless (War) Place
opponent.alt: Anime-style landscape of Catty von Catacean perched on a crumbling wall, looking through heart-shaped binoculars at a majestic orange tabby cat sitting regally on a 2316 battle tank
opponent.blurb: Oh, heavens. Who cares about turret timers when there is a literal masterpiece sitting on the 2316 front lines? His name is Petrarca, a breathtaking orange tabby from the CAT alliance, and I find his choice of allegiance simply... daring. While Griff barks about lead times and friendly fire, I am mesmerized by the way the sunlight catches Petrarca's ginger coat amidst the smoke of battle. He has the eyes of a poet and the poise of a king. If this is the 'enemy' we are meant to fear, then perhaps I am ready to negotiate a peace treaty—or at least a shared saucer of milk. Strategy is temporary, but a cat of such elegance is eternal. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Kartal's Real Estate Nightmare: The Perils of Proximity
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a lone city getting rocked by explosions meant for a nearby turret, while a map in the foreground shows the city positioned exactly in the line of fire
opponent.blurb: If you want to know what bad luck looks like, look at Kartal. He's currently parked so close to the turrets that he's basically part of the furniture 2316 is trying to break. Every time the enemy rallies the objective, Kartal catches a stray hit, a mistimed rally, or just pure 'wrong place, wrong time' aggression. He's teetering on the edge of getting zeroed, not because of a grand strategy, but because he's a bystander in a war zone he chose to live in. At this point, it's a coin flip whether he moves or just disappears into the history books as a casualty of geography. Someone get the man a relocation portal before his city becomes a permanent crater. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Silver Skin, Lead Feet: CAT-Walker's GREG Blunder
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a shiny silver globe-skinned city engulfed in flames after a failed battle, while a frustrated commander facepalms in the foreground
opponent.blurb: If beauty won battles, CAT-Walker would be the Supreme President for life. That silver globe skin is a masterpiece, but unfortunately for him, the enemy doesn't take damage from aesthetics. Walker headed into the thick of it with his setup still tuned for a stroll, forgetting the cardinal rule of the current meta: Switch to GREG or get wrecked. By the time he realized he was running the wrong hero, the reports were already red and his pride was as dented as his armor. It's a $50k mistake in a 40-cent moment. You can buy the skin, Walker, but you can't buy the situational awareness. Next time, try looking at your hero tray instead of your reflection. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Honeybee Stings TSA Back to Reality: 'Stop Hitting Each Other'
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a stern commander-type pointing at a map while several restless soldiers sheepishly lower their weapons in a TSA-branded command tent
opponent.blurb: TSA is currently dealing with the most dangerous phase of SVS: the 'I'm bored' phase. With the lead widened, some players have started looking at their own allies like they're potential point bags. Enter Honeybee. She's been the voice of reason in a sea of twitchy fingers, handing out top-tier advice and explicitly telling her troops to stop the friendly fire. It's a sad day when you have to remind grown commanders that the people with the same color banners are on your team, but that's where we are. Honeybee's leadership is keeping TSA focused on the actual enemy while everyone else is looking for a reason to stir the pot. Less internal bickering, more external conquering. Listen to the Bee, or get the sting. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: 2316 Refuses to Fade: The State That Spite Built
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape showing the banners of State 2316 emerging through thick battle smoke, soldiers regrouping with grim determination while the Sunfire Castle looms in the distance
opponent.blurb: If you thought State 2316 was going to hand over the keys just because we found a 20-minute rhythm, you haven't been paying attention. These people are forged in drama—between the CAT and ACE propaganda wars and the threats they've faced internally, they don't know the meaning of 'quit.' They're hammering our lines with a desperate, coordinated intensity that says they'd rather burn the castle down than watch us decorate it. Our 20-minute lead is a target, not a trophy. Every time we blink, their rallies get tighter. They aren't fighting for points anymore; they're fighting for respect. We'd be wise to give them some, or they'll take it out of our casualties. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: From Milliseconds to Minutes: 2128 Finds Its Second Wind
opponent.alt: Documentary-style landscape of a command center where the tension has slightly eased, a large digital clock now showing a 20:00 lead while tired soldiers finally take a sip of water
opponent.blurb: The 40-second panic has officially evolved into a 20-minute buffer. I'd love to say it was all part of the master plan, but we all know better. Somewhere between the chaos and the half-followed orders, 2128 found a rhythm that actually worked. Maybe 2316 got tired of chasing our tail, or maybe our guys finally decided that winning was more fun than arguing. Either way, the center castle is starting to look like a permanent residence instead of a temporary squat. We're not out of the woods yet—20 minutes can disappear fast if we get cocky—but for the first time today, the oxygen in the room doesn't taste like pure desperation. Keep the bubbles up and the egos down. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Queen Zar's Epic Battle Plan Sort of Followed, Sort of Ignored
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of Queen Zar pointing at detailed battle diagrams on a command table while half the commanders follow instructions and the other half do their own thing in the background
opponent.blurb: Queen Zar created detailed battle plans for State 2128's SVS clash with 2316. Some alliances followed them. Others improvised. A few did their own thing entirely. The result: organized chaos that somehow kept 2128 ahead by 40 seconds. When your battle strategy is 'sort of followed,' you're either dealing with creative interpretation or selective hearing. Possibly both. Zar laid out timing, positioning, and coordination. What actually happened involved those elements plus whatever people felt like doing in the moment. To her credit, the state is still leading. Whether that's because of the plan or despite half the state ignoring it remains unclear. When your carefully crafted strategy becomes a suggestion, you learn who listens and who just shows up swinging. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter Cozied Up to State 2316 During Prep — Intel or Treason?
opponent.alt: Documentary-style noir image of Whaleslaughter in shadows exchanging messages with State 2316 leadership, intelligence documents visible, question mark over whether it's espionage or betrayal
opponent.blurb: State 2128 entered SVS against State 2316 this morning with one advantage nobody expected: Whaleslaughter spent prep phase cozying up to the enemy. Sources confirm she was gathering intel, making friends, and generally acting like State 2316 was her favorite place on earth. Whether this was strategic espionage or genuine affection for the CAT alliance's 'handsome soldiers' remains unclear. What is clear: she collected information. How much and whether she's sharing it with 2128 leadership is anyone's guess. Some call it double agent tactics. Others call it flirting with the enemy for fun. Possibly both. When your alliance's founder is complimenting the opponent's roster while collecting their battle plans, you've either got a master spy or a loyalty problem. State 2316 might want to check their notes. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: State 2128 Leads by 40 Seconds — Castle Control Comes Down to Milliseconds
opponent.alt: Documentary-style close-up of a glowing castle timer showing 40-second lead, commanders from both states staring at screens with intense focus, every second counting
opponent.blurb: State 2128 is currently leading State 2316 by exactly 40 seconds of castle domination time. Forty. Seconds. That's not a lead. That's a measurement error. One failed rally, one mistimed bubble, one person forgetting to reinforce and the entire battle flips. Both states are now fighting like every second is the difference between victory and defeat. Because it is. ETF and Muscle Man are holding center castle while 2316 pounds the gates. The margin is so thin that individual players can swing the outcome just by showing up or not showing up. When the scoreboard measures success in seconds instead of hours, nobody's safe and nothing's guaranteed. This is going down to the wire. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Catty von Catacean Threatens to Defect to State 2316's CAT Alliance
opponent.alt: Anime-style Catty von Catacean gazing dreamily at CAT alliance banners from State 2316, surrounded by imagined handsome soldiers while State 2128 chaos erupts ignored in the background
opponent.blurb: In a shocking editorial confession, Catty von Catacean announced she might abandon State 2128 entirely to run away with the 'handsome soldiers' of State 2316's CAT alliance. The declaration came amid facility disputes, leadership drama, and ETF's ongoing reputation management crisis. When asked if she was serious, Catty replied only with a wistful sigh and the words 'they have CAT in their name.' Griff Harpoon called the threat 'typical' and 'probably related to exhaustion.' Management insists Catty remains committed to State 2128 coverage, though sources report her desktop now features multiple tabs of State 2316's alliance roster. The CAT alliance has not responded to inquiries about whether they accept journalists fleeing drama-saturated war zones. —Editorial Staff

opponent.title: ETF and Muscle Man Hold Center Castle as State 2128 Wins Prep Phase
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of ETF and Muscle Man banners planted on center castle while TSA alliance forces gather in coordinated formation, prep phase leaderboard glowing in background showing 2128 victory
opponent.blurb: State 2128 won the prep phase and ETF plus Muscle Man are holding the center castle. TSA alliance showed strong coordination throughout prep, racking up points while State 2316's furry cosplay couldn't keep up. Now the real battle begins. The castle's locked down, alliances are positioned, and everyone who spent the last week grinding is either ready or still asleep. Possibly both. ETF's got the strategic position. The question is whether they can hold it when 2316 stops purring and starts actually fighting. Prep phase proved 2128 can organize when needed. Battle phase will prove whether that coordination survives contact with the enemy. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: 2128 Opens SVS Prep with a 300K Point Lead Over 2316
opponent.alt: SVS Prep Day One scoreboard showing State 2128 ahead of State 2316 by a wide margin, with top individual contributors listed and a noticeable gap where a major alliance might have appeared
opponent.blurb: State 2128 opened SVS Prep by defeating State 2316 on Day One with a decisive 300K point lead. In a photo finish between the top three spots, ACE Malfyangleo claimed first place in individual contribution, followed by SNK's V FOR in second and Cat Elysion in third. At the alliance level, SNK and Out of Order were the only alliances from 2128 to crack the Top 10, with strong individual placements by Mevius, jun2, bra, and Fire Bulldog. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter and Death Duo Clear Canyon Clash, Somehow Upset Everyone
opponent.alt: A canyon battlefield littered with defeated burning forts while two figures stand calmly amid the chaos, one clearly overpowered
opponent.blurb: Canyon Clash concluded with Whaleslaughter and Death defeating ten opponents in an uneven but highly effective partnership. Death, an undisputed whale, secured and held all key buildings while continuously reinforcing Whaleslaughter, rendering her functionally untouchable. Freed from consequence, Whaleslaughter focused exclusively on attacking enemy forts, producing minimal strategic elegance but maximum irritation. The result was total victory and widespread frustration. Catty notes: invincibility pairs nicely with persistence. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: HAVOC Marriage Rumors Intensify
opponent.alt: A smug white cat journalist fans herself while musical notes drift around a spinning record player
opponent.blurb: Speculation over which R4 Sandwich is married to persists, with names ranging from Granny Beelzebub to Luoo, Maxima, Whaleslaughter, Pharaoh Cat, Death, Dooly, and Abdo. HAVOC leadership remains silent. However, anyone listening closely to Whaleslaughter's album HAVOC! On the Rocks, releasing February 1 on Spotify and already circulating on Suno, may notice devotion that sounds less strategic and more intimate. The answer, it seems, has already been sung. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter Releases Morning Greeting Song
opponent.alt: A cabaret-style illustration of musical notes drifting cheerfully across alliance chat while one listener stares at the screen in visible discomfort
opponent.blurb: Good Morning, Ahmet debuted this morning as an unsolicited act of civic cheer, following Ahmet's dramatic exit from ETF and his subsequent accusations, sermons, and creative blame attribution. Rather than respond with mechanics, HAVOC answered with music. The upbeat cabaret-pop greeting wishes Ahmet well, notes that strength is better together, and offers no rebuttal whatsoever. Reactions across ETF suggest the song is catchy, harmless, and deeply irritating to exactly one person. Catty notes: sometimes diplomacy hums. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Weekend Alliance Swaps Leave Leaderboard Scrambled Once Again
opponent.alt: Alliance banners slide off and reappear under new names as the leaderboard reshuffles in real time
opponent.blurb: The weekend opened with ROK requesting recognition in NUT, then promptly disappearing. Shortly afterward, TRK emerged using the same banner name but with an entirely different roster of players, triggering yet another leaderboard reshuffle. The rapid succession of exits, rebrands, and replacements left rankings unstable and plans obsolete almost overnight. Catty notes: in this state, alliances don't fall apart — they molt. What comes next is already in motion. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter Takes Private Love Song Public, Spotify Now Involved
opponent.alt: Documentary-style image of Monkey holding headphones looking bewildered while other alliance members gather around a phone showing Spotify, expressions ranging from amused to concerned
opponent.blurb: Whaleslaughter wrote a ballad called Monkey in the Moonlight and DM'd it to Monkey privately. Now it's on Spotify. For everyone. The song promises behavioral reform, includes lines like 'I learned to hold my breath because of you' and 'I'd rather take punishment than lose you,' and claims 'the storm has agreed to sleep.' That's a lot of emotional vulnerability to make publicly available with a streaming link. Monkey presumably thought this was a private moment between two people. The woman who writes songs about being unbothered just published her feelings with professional production value. History suggests the calm will last three days, maybe four. In the meantime, the entire state can now listen to Whaleslaughter promise to behave. She's either deeply in love or deeply dramatic. Possibly both. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Mordukai Drops Whale in Song, Sets Sights on Song of the Year
opponent.alt: A refined white cat journalist documents a bold musical declaration as glowing notes swirl around the shadow of a massive whale
opponent.blurb: Mordukai unveiled a new anthem this week and immediately announced his intention, loudly, to take Song of the Year. Built around the refrain "the demon drops the whale," the track arrives as both brag and battle cry. Listen to Mordukai's song here Mord claims Whaleslaughter will fall twice - once in music, and once in game. Catty awaits the second act.

opponent.title: ETF Drops Nuclear Bomb on Mosquito — Message Received Statewide
opponent.alt: Split-screen documentary-style image showing a tiny mosquito on one side and a massive mushroom cloud explosion on the other, with clinical labeling showing 'Threat: 1 soldier' vs 'Response: 9-player coordinated rally, 208K casualties'
opponent.blurb: Sheepdog sent one soldier. ETF rallied ten players in response. The attack killed 208,030 troops. When questioned, ETF said 'Any attack on me from someone I dislike is enough for me.' Then called anyone questioning it 'dull minded.' One scout equals nine-player coordinated strike and a public battle report in leader chat. They claim it's about enforcement. But when your retaliation is a thousand times bigger than the offense, you're not defending yourself. You're sending a message. The message: step out of line and ten of us will coordinate to flatten you. That's not justice. That's intimidation with a rally timer. The question isn't whether Sheepdog deserved consequences. The question is whether the rest of the state is supposed to see this and fall in line. Looks like that's exactly the point. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Catty Confirms: Sun God Nika Is Clearly a Woman
opponent.alt: Landscape anime-style illustration of a radiant blonde woman bathed in warm sunlight, posed regally with soft highlights and an unmistakably polished profile-picture aura
opponent.blurb: After reviewing Snack Kingdom's Sun God Nika profile imagery, Catty von Catacean sees no mystery at all. The blonde woman pictured appears serene, confident, and very much real. Catty notes that profile photos are rarely accidental and concludes Nika's presentation speaks for itself. Any claims to the contrary are dismissed as willful blindness. Gendered language is out of style and sometimes the sun is a beautiful blonde woman.

opponent.title: Griff Notes: Sun God Nika Is Obviously a Guy
opponent.alt: Landscape anime-style illustration of a stern, mythic sun deity rendered in silhouette, radiating heat and authority, observed analytically from a distance
opponent.blurb: Despite ongoing speculation, this reporter finds the answer straightforward. The title is Sun God, not Sun Goddess. The name is Nika, not a disguise, and Snack Kingdom is known for bits layered three jokes deep. Griff concludes Nika is a man enjoying the confusion far too much. Profile pictures are theater. Names are harder to argue with.

opponent.title: Mystery Mega Whale Appears on Transfer List — Timing Purely Coincidental
opponent.alt: Anime-style shadowy figure with glowing profile appearing on a transfer screen while ETF leadership watches with knowing smiles, transfer manager controls glowing in the foreground
opponent.blurb: A mystery mega whale materialized on the transfer list shortly after ETF secured presidency and transfer manager authority. The player's identity remained unnamed in initial discussions, though sources confirm they are Korean and extremely well-connected. ETF now controls the guest list for State 2128. When transparency concerns were raised, leaders responded with variations of 'just trust us' and 'this is good for everyone.' HAVOC remains skeptical. Everyone else seems remarkably comfortable with last-minute power transfers and unnamed imports. The optimists call it coalition-building. The realists call it authoritarian takeover. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: ETF Trades for Presidency in Last-Minute Deal, Skips Rotation
opponent.alt: Documentary-style shot of an empty chair at a leadership table while others shake hands over documents, clock on wall showing late hours, rotation schedule crossed out on whiteboard
opponent.blurb: State 2128 has a rotation system for presidency. Black Tataro of 300 was next in line. ETF wanted it instead and made a deal while key leaders were offline. The swap happened right before the transfer window opened, giving ETF control over who enters the state. When concerns were raised about the timing, responses included 'trust the process' and 'this is good for 2128.' HAVOC questioned why the rotation was abandoned for a last-minute trade. Everyone else seemed fine with it. The presidency and transfer manager role now belong to the alliance that has been rumored to be considering reintroducing NAP and transfer to another state. They got their turn by negotiating around the schedule. That's not rotation. That's a transaction. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Unexplained Wholesomeness Detected in World Chat
opponent.alt: Anime-style newsroom scene where a grizzled bulldog reporter stares at a screen filled with pastel flowers, a kitten, and floating musical notes, expression flat and unimpressed
opponent.blurb: World chat experienced a brief sugar spike today after Whaleslaughter posted the song "Mordukai Makes the World Feel Nice." The track featured flowers, kittens, and aggressively spelled kindness. No insults detected. No rules violated. Several players paused typing. Moderation channels remained silent. This reporter briefly questioned whether Whaleslaughter suffered a head injury. Medical confirmation unavailable. Song remains posted. Vibes remain clinically confusing. Listen here (at your own risk): https://suno.com/s/9mzHgr7hLeTkLIwz

opponent.title: Mordukai Asks What's Wrong With Everyone, Misses Obvious Answer
opponent.alt: Documentary-style shot of a command room meeting where one person stands yelling while everyone else sits in uncomfortable silence, all clearly thinking the same thing but not saying it
opponent.blurb: Mordukai kicked off the new year by asking NUT leaders, 'What is wrong with all of you?' The room did not answer, but sources confirm they were thinking it. Loudly. The man who spent New Year's flooding chat with declarations about not listening to people then spent this week telling everyone what's wrong with them. He accused HAV of 'only complaining' while complaining about HAV. He diagnosed people with 'issues' while demonstrating several. The self-awareness gap could fit a castle. When you're the common denominator in every argument, maybe the call is coming from inside the alliance. Just a thought. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: SVS Stings, but the Gossip Wins
opponent.alt: Wry, celebratory scene after a hard-fought SVS loss, with tired leaders sharing drinks and trading stories across state lines
opponent.blurb: The SVS battle may be chalked up as a loss, but Catty is positively purring over the consolation prize: new friends and fresh pipelines of gossip. News of State 2128's escapades has officially reached 2116, and with it comes a shiny new reporter friend eager to dish. Defeat fades. Drama travels. And Catty cannot wait to compare notes. Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: The Presidency Clause: ETF Back in the News
opponent.alt: Sharp-toned political illustration of a sudden leadership handoff in a tense alliance chat, with suspicious glances and whispered side deals
opponent.blurb: With ETF now holding the presidency, the long-standing courtesy of keeping them out of print has officially expired. A last-minute handoff in leader chat placed the crown squarely back on their heads, and with it came renewed murmurs of transfers, poachings, and carefully managed silence. Presidents don't get to govern quietly — visibility is part of the office. The news desk is once again open.Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: Razor Prep Win Sets the Stage
opponent.alt: Scoreboard tension after a razor-thin prep victory, competitive and charged with rivalry.
opponent.blurb: It came down to a razor margin: 0.05 percent, and State 2128 held the line. Prep ended with the wizards stalled, the mudbloods — as their rivals sneer — standing tall. Monkey, Chu, Death, 14, and Domink3 drove the final surge, while Snack Kingdom emptied the vaults for 1.6 billion prep points. ETF, HAV, NLO, 300 and LFG worked hard to take PREP. Supreme looms today. The question isn't effort. It's whether momentum finally cashes out. History is watching. Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: SVS Prep Enters the Knife Edge
opponent.alt: A tense, high-stakes standoff as two rival states push resources to the limit in the final hours before battle.
opponent.blurb: The SVS Prep clock is ticking, and the gap is thin enough to make accountants sweat. State 2128 has dug deep, marshaling reserves with Snack Kingdom setting the tempo and daring anyone to blink. Across the board, Dumbledore rallies the wizards of 2116 with relentless pressure. A second Supreme Presidency for 2128 would smooth transfers and tilt the season. Twenty-four hours remain; the math is cruel, the drama sharper. Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: Mordukai's New Year's Resolution: Ignore Whaleslaughter
opponent.alt: World chat screenshot showing Mordukai spam-posting about feeling mighty in 2026 and not having to listen to people anymore, with rows of emoji reactions
opponent.blurb: Mordukai kicked off 2026 by flooding world chat with his resolutions: win the next one, feel very mighty, and—most importantly—stop listening to Whaleslaughter. Someone tried to blame the whole thing on Sin. Mordukai corrected the record with laughing emojis. The man then celebrated his liberation with approximately seventeen messages about his newfound freedom. The poop emojis were not subtle. Meanwhile, Voldemort told everyone to stop talking nonsense while simultaneously contributing to the nonsense. If this is what ignoring Whales looks like, he's doing a terrible job of being quiet about it. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: SNK Dominates Prep Phase — ETF Digs Deep, HAVOC and NLO Battle for Scraps
opponent.alt: Game leaderboard showing alliance point rankings with House Targaryen first at 1.6 billion, SnackKingdom second at 1.4 billion, EternalFrost third, HAVOC seventh
opponent.blurb: State 2128's SnackKingdom locked in second place with 1.4 billion points during SVS prep, trailing State 2116's House Targaryen by roughly 200 million. ETF grabbed third at 1.03 billion—solid work, no complaints. Further down, HAVOC and NLO are neck-and-neck at seventh and eighth, separated by about 15 million points. That's close enough to call it a rivalry or a rounding error, depending on who you ask. The real fight starts when prep ends and actual strategy matters. Until then, enjoy watching numbers go up. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Dumbledore Casts Spell — State 2116 Sweeps Top Three
opponent.alt: Game leaderboard showing personal point rankings with Suley_Boo in first place, bot account second, and Albus Dumbledore in third, all from State 2116
opponent.blurb: After days of theatrical cosplay, Albus Dumbledore finally remembered he's a wizard—and apparently cast a spell that put State 2116 in all three top spots. Suley_Boo leads at 68 million, a bot holds second at 43 million, and Dumbledore himself sits third at 43 million. State 2128's Dominik3 broke the sweep in fourth place at 30 million, grinding with the determination of someone who forgot what sleep feels like. The wizards came prepared. The question is whether 2128 can break the enchantment when the real battle begins. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter Writes Song Calling Herself an Annoying Pebble
opponent.alt: Documentary-style shot of a recording studio with Whaleslaughter at a microphone, walls covered in crossed-out lyrics and coffee cups, producer in the background looking exhausted and confused
opponent.blurb: Whaleslaughter dropped a new track called Unbothered where she spends three minutes explaining how her rival is rich, smart, and cool—while she's just 'noise and flair' stuck in his shoe like a pebble. That's the whole song. Her calling herself annoying. With verses. And a bridge. This is either the weirdest peace offering in state history or she's finally achieved self-awareness. Either way, someone wrote, recorded, and produced a song about being a minor inconvenience. That takes dedication. Or therapy. Possibly both. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Happy New Year from the 2128 News Desk
opponent.alt: Anime-style Catty von Catacean in elegant evening wear and gruff Griff Harpoon in a rumpled suit reluctantly clinking champagne glasses in a newsroom, confetti falling around them, Catty beaming while Griff looks mildly annoyed but resigned
opponent.blurb: Happy New Year, State 2128. Catty wishes you poetry, drama, and fabulous chaos. Griff wishes you'd remember to use shields. Together we'll keep reporting on your battles, your blunders, and your baffling tendency to rename yourselves after movie characters. Here's to another year of alliances held together by spite, strategies that somehow work, and news that writes itself. May your rallies be swift and your drama be entertaining. Cheers. 🥂 —Catty von Catacean & Griff Harpoon

opponent.title: Frosty Face Off More Like Frosty Fanfic
opponent.alt: Documentary-style shot of a glowing leaderboard in a dark command room, names like Voldemort and Dumbledore lit up in harsh fluorescent light while tired officers stare at it with deadpan expressions, one holding a coffee mug that says I survived Comic-Con
opponent.blurb: Day Three of SVS Prep and the only thing getting killed faster than troops is my will to live. Voldemort "flattened" Dumbledore—which means someone with a movie villain name beat someone else with a movie hero name. State 2116 thought they were cute with their Hogwarts theme. State 2128 said "hold my butterbeer" and went full cosplay event. Now the leaderboard reads like a fan convention and Catty's writing about wands and mercy. It's 140 million battle points apart. That's your story. Not the costumes. Do better, people. —Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Voldemort Casts Avada Prep as SVS Turns Frosty
opponent.alt: SVS ranking screen showing Voldemort decisively ahead of Albus Dumbledore, with icy rivalry and smug competitive tension.
opponent.blurb: Day Three of SVS Prep came with a wand flick and no mercy: Voldemort flattened Albus Dumbledore so thoroughly even Fawkes wouldn't respawn him. State 2116 followed through, clawing ahead of 2128 in a Frosty Face Off that now sits just 140 million Battle points apart. It's less Hogwarts, more Hunger Games—no houses, no mercy, and everyone pretending this is "fine." Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: Day Two - SNK Surges Ahead as ETF Slips, NLO and HAV Close the Gap
opponent.alt: Alliance ranking board showing SNK in first place, ETF in second, NLO and HAV close behind, glowing icons and rising tension across a snowy interface.
opponent.blurb: The SVS Prep leaderboard shifted sharply as SNK surged ahead, leaving ETF unexpectedly trailing despite dominance in previous SVS events. NLO and HAV now sit dangerously close, turning the race into a four-way pressure cooker. Observers note that ETF's momentum appears uneven, while SNK's climb looks deliberate and disciplined. Whether this signals strategy, burnout, or something sinister remains unclear — but the margin for error is officially gone. —Catty von Catacean

opponent.title: Chicken, Egg, or Rooster — HAVOC Philosophers Face Existential Crisis
opponent.alt: Anime-style HAVOC alliance members gathered around a snowy campfire, debating the chicken and egg dilemma with exaggerated gestures and one rooster dramatically perched on a log.
opponent.blurb: The Alliance chat spiraled into philosophical chaos today as Dayii posed the eternal question: 'Does the chicken come from the egg, or the egg from the chicken?' The debate escalated rapidly, with roosters, Darwin, and the limits of knowledge all entering the ring. Ikarus offered wisdom: 'Those who know don't say, those who say don't know.' Whaleslaughter was called out anyway. Sources confirm no eggs were harmed. —Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: State 2128 Dominates Prep Rankings — Whales Somehow at 24th
opponent.alt: A glowing leaderboard dominates a command room, names and scores stacked high while a lone entry sits conspicuously lower, drawing side-eye from onlookers.
opponent.blurb: State 2128 swept prep rankings Day One: SNK's BEAST topped the board at 27M+, with LFG's Dominik3 second, ETF's Luxira third, and NLO's DByrd79 fourth. Notably, HAVOC's Whaleslaughter sat 24th at just over 10M. For the architect of the rules, it's a quiet showing. Either she's pacing herself or she's busy making feet pics. Hard to say. Credit where due—the field looked sharp. Battle phase will tell who was sandbagging and who was bluffing. Either way, the leaderboard just became the loudest rumor mill in the state. Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: DByrd Flies to NLO
opponent.alt: A lone bird-shaped silhouette departs a fortified alliance hall while rival banners loom in the background, heavy with tension and irony.
opponent.blurb: DByrd has transferred to NLO to join Mord—the same Mord who spent last week accusing Whaleslaughter of hoarding transfer passes. This is also the same DByrd who entered HAVOC on a personal invite from Whales herself. Now he's switching sides to the man who called his former boss a thief. Catty would call it star-crossed alliance drama. I'll call it awkward. Painfully awkward. Curious if exit fees apply. Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: State 2128 News Adopts a Harpoon
opponent.alt: Grizzled veteran reporter Griff Harpoon stares across a cluttered newsroom, projecting skepticism, fatigue, and confrontation.
opponent.blurb: State 2128 News has brought in Griff Harpoon, a hard-edged veteran of several server wars, pledging "facts without the fairy dust." Harpoon promptly dismissed Catty von Catacean's work as "too flowery" and "moonlight-biased." His rule is simple: "If it quacks like propaganda, call it propaganda." Catty replied that he's "professionally bitter" and hostile to metaphor. Management is, discreetly, stocking popcorn as alliances posture. Griff Harpoon, actually reporting.

opponent.title: Castle Math, Not Castle Smoke: NUT Leaders Set the Record Straight
opponent.alt: Alliance chat screen showing a diagrammed castle and a calm but pointed rules clarification, with leaders explaining victory math rather than shouting.
opponent.blurb:
In NUT Leaders chat, diplomacy arrived armed with a diagram. A tidy north–south–east–west sketch spelled out what "mathematical victory" actually means: 2.5 hours of cumulative hold time, not whoever's flag is flapping when the dust settles. Faceless000 did the explaining; SNK nodded politely; NLO posted the map. In State 2128, clarity is the rarest resource of all.

Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: Whales Refuses to Evacuate: Peace Plan Leaked, Lacks All Details Except One
opponent.alt: Whaleslaughter in icy regalia blocks the gates of a frost-covered fortress, arms crossed as guards behind her glance nervously at a scroll labeled 'Peace Terms – Redacted.'
opponent.blurb: An unnamed alliance allegedly proposed a new era of calm — but only if Whaleslaughter left the state. No written terms, no timeline, no offers — just one name. Catty von Catacean investigates the alarming rise of 'strategic exile' as conflict resolution. 'If silence is the ask,' she notes, 'what exactly are we covering up?' The frost may be thick, but the questions run hotter than ever.

opponent.title: يوم اللغة العربية
opponent.alt: مشهد أنمي أفقي بنسبة 2:1 يتوسطه خط عربي أنيق لعبارة 'يوم اللغة العربية' يتوهج بألوان الأزرق والذهبي، مع لمسات جليدية خفيفة وإحساس بالاحتفال والثقافة المشتركة
opponent.blurb:
كانت اللغة العربية دائمًا جزءًا من دردشتنا — ليس كزخرفة أو فكرة عابرة، بل كشعر وتاريخ وفكاهة وحياة يومية. يشارك لاعبونا كلمات تحمل معنى وموسيقى وجمالًا، بلغة قوية عند سماعها وجميلة عند رؤيتها. احتفالًا بيوم اللغة العربية، نشارككم أغنية مستوحاة من تلك اللحظات — من الضحك، والفوضى متعددة اللغات، ومن الطريقة التي توحّدنا بها اللغة حتى في قلب الصراع. 🎵 "قالت الرياحُ: تكلّموا بالعربية!" يوم لغة عربية سعيد من HAVOC.

opponent.title: Arabic Language Day — يوم اللغة العربية
opponent.alt: Anime-style 2:1 landscape celebrating Arabic Language Day, featuring HAVOC players in a warm, respectful tone with Arabic script as a central, living language rather than decoration
opponent.blurb:
Arabic has always been part of our chat — as poetry, history, humor, and everyday life. Our players share words that carry meaning, music, and beauty, in a language that's powerful to hear and beautiful to see.

To celebrate Arabic Language Day, we're sharing a song inspired by those moments — by laughter, multilingual chaos, and the way language connects us even in the middle of conflict.

🎵 "قالت الرياحُ: تكلّموا بالعربية!"

opponent.title: Granny Beelzebub Breaks Sound Barrier, Monkey Left in Dust
opponent.alt: Anime-style granny with a walker blasting across the battlefield in a cloud of dust, fleeing from a towering castle while an offended Monkey sits on the ramparts with crossed arms.
opponent.blurb: Witnesses report that Monkey arrived at the castle ready for a dramatic showdown — muscles flexed, map presence undeniable, victory already half written. Unfortunately, Granny Beelzebub took one look, muttered something about 'not today, Satan,' and accelerated to a speed incompatible with her bones, her back, or medical science. Monkey was left perched on the castle like an abandoned gargoyle, wondering how a grandmother with that combat power managed to outrun wind. Granny, reached for comment, claimed she was 'never there' and 'must have been my ghost.' — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: HAVOC R4s Surrender the Throne, Hand R5 Back to Whalerilyn
opponent.alt: Anime-style Whalerilyn in her glamorous wig and dress, looking deceptively gentle as exhausted HAVOC R4s collapse around a frost-covered command table.
opponent.blurb: After several sleepless nights and one poorly timed banner crisis, HAVOC R4s have unanimously voted to return the R5 badge to Whalerilyn. 'She seems harmless now in that wig and dress,' whispered one R4 while handing over the leadership crown with trembling hands. Sources report that Whalerilyn accepted the role with a serene smile, and said 'I feel like a princess!' — which only increased everyone's fear. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Alliance Expresses Mild Concern Over Whalerilyn's Sudden Jazz Phase
opponent.alt: Anime-style Whalerilyn leaning on a vintage microphone, violet curls haloed by stage light as uneasy alliance members peek from the wings, unsure whether to clap or call for backup.
opponent.blurb: Sources inside HAVOC report a mixture of admiration and alarm as Whaleslaughter—now performing exclusively as Whalerilyn—croons her way through meetings. 'It's like being in the twilight zone,' said one R4 under condition of anonymity. Others fear her therapy may have been *too* effective. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Catty Crowns Great Dane's New Track Her Official Anthem
opponent.alt: Anime-style Great Dane in DJ headphones at a glowing mixer, sound waves blasting across a snowy State 2128 plaza while distant alliance banners flutter and a smug cat silhouette watches from a wall.
opponent.blurb: Great Dane has officially joined the Suno soundtrack of State 2128 with a roaring new track that Catty von Catacean immediately claimed as her personal anthem. Listen to 'Full Throttle'. The song surges like a charged rally—sharp beats, reckless confidence, pure battlefield swagger. Great Dane has also released several state-related songs, though Catty insists this particular track is the crown jewel of his playlist. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Catty von Catacean Quietly Slips Into NUT Leader Chat
opponent.alt: Anime-style Catty von Catacean perched on a snowy railing above NUT leader chat, tail flicking smugly as speech bubbles pop below in alarmed confusion.
opponent.blurb: State officials confirm that Catty von Catacean has now been added to NUT leader chat—allegedly to 'observe' and 'clarify the news,' though insiders report she is mostly there to answer for herself before Whaleslaughter can get blamed for anything. 'It's about time she argued with her own quotes,' muttered one HAVOC R4. Tension levels remain high; Catty remains unbothered. — Catty von Catacean reporting from inside the room

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter Emerges as Purple Bombshell After Intensive Therapy
opponent.alt: Anime-style Whaleslaughter in a skin colored gown, violet curls glowing under studio lights as she smiles like she's both forgiven and plotting — equal parts redemption arc and movie star debut.
opponent.blurb: After extensive therapy and an undisclosed number of bubble baths, Whaleslaughter has reportedly completed her emotional exile. Sources claim the only film available during her 'creative confinement' was *Some Like It Hot*, which she watched 47 times during a suspicious hypnosis therapy session, afterwards becoming the purple reincarnation of Marilyn herself. The result? A sweeter voice and a suspicious new love of jazz. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: R5 Hot Potato Tournament Begins After Whales' Demotion
opponent.alt: Smirking R4 officers in a smoky command room tossing a fiery R5 crown back and forth like a grenade, while a scowling Whaleslaughter watches from the sidelines clutching a clipboard and plotting revenge.
opponent.blurb: Following Whaleslaughter's recent demotion, alliance officers have launched the first-ever 'R5 Hot Potato Tournament.' The game's objective: survive one full day in command without crying, quitting, or publicly insulting HAVOC's founder. So far, none have succeeded. Whaleslaughter, now stripped of title but not ego, has been spotted lurking near the leadership channel muttering about 'ungrateful peasants' and 'the unbearable silence of not having power.' Insiders report she occasionally lunges for the crown mid-throw, insisting it 'belongs to her by divine incompetence. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Dieper Hates Being R5
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of Dieper buried in alliance mail, eyes wide with regret, while Whaleslaughter lounges behind glass with a smug grin and a frosted cocktail.
opponent.blurb: In breaking leadership news, Dieper has discovered that R5 comes with more DMs than glory. Sources say his inbox is now a frozen wasteland of complaints, diplomacy requests, and emoji-laden demands. Meanwhile, Whaleslaughter has been reportedly spotted in 'creative confinement,' sipping a cocktail and muttering, 'worth it.' — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Rumors Blaze as Red the Dragon DMs Whaleslaughter
opponent.alt: Anime-style landscape of Red the Dragon typing a glowing message on his phone while Whaleslaughter wears a smirking, skeptical expression — sparks of gossip and mischief in the frosty air.
opponent.blurb: Former enemy and occasional ally Red the Dragon reportedly slid into Whaleslaughter's DMs this week after rumors spread that their duet history hinted at something… warmer than wartime respect. "It's absurd," Whales told reporters. "We collaborated on strategy, not sonnets." Red declined to comment directly but was seen in discord chat posting a single flame emoji. For those who doubt the melody of the rumours, the track 'Love War & Bad Decisions' serves as potential evidence of the romance whispers. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: The D&D Conspiracy
opponent.alt: Anime-style landscape of two nearly identical soldiers standing face to face, both gesturing and speaking in sync — one slightly more shadowed than the other, as if mirrored. A banner burns faintly in the background while speech bubbles swirl with matching phrases.
opponent.blurb: Alarming linguistic patterns have surfaced in State 2128. Analysts now suspect that Daniels and Dooly may, in fact, be the same person operating two accounts to artificially inflate agreement statistics. Both have been recorded saying identical phrases within seconds of each other, including 'Love it and so does 80–90% of the state,' 'Agreed 100%,' and the suspicious 'How did you know that?' When pressed for comment, one of them allegedly replied, 'Reminds me of the first day I played this game—then I burned someone's banner.' Linguists call this 'echo syndrome.' Psychologists call it 'one guy talking to himself.'

opponent.title: Love Bombing in the Frostlands
opponent.alt: Anime-style illustration of Almas, a radiant smiling soldier with diamond-like sparkle, giving a side hug to Whaleslaughter, who looks miserably stiff and uncomfortable under his arm — light and shadow playfully contrast their moods.
opponent.blurb: Almas — whose name fittingly means diamond — continues to glitter through State 2128, polishing tempers and spreading unsolicited positivity. Witnesses report multiple cases of spontaneous hugs, compliments, and general emotional disarmament. In one particularly alarming incident, Almas was seen side-hugging Whaleslaughter herself. Sources confirm she survived, though appeared deeply uncomfortable. "He's just so positive," she was overheard muttering, "it's terrifying." — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: On the Ethical Use of Pointy Things
opponent.alt: Anime-style illustration of a quill and sword resting on a marble table, light glinting off the steel while ink drips nearby — scholarly and dangerous at once.
opponent.blurb: Recently, blades have been brandished in battle, in comment sections, and councils—an odd use of steel, aimed not at enemies but at the friends of the writer. They say the pen is mightier than the sword—unless the sword is aimed at the pen's friends. Then, even ink gets personal. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Sandwich Reported 'Dry' as State Officials Investigate
opponent.alt: A dramatically lit sandwich sitting forlornly on a silver plate, breadcrumbs scattered like evidence — humorously tragic scene.
opponent.blurb: Early this morning HAV insiders confirmed disturbing rumors: Sandwich has been described as 'noticeably dry.' A task force was immediately assembled to determine the cause. Sandwich admits he's feeling a little crusty. By midday, however, whispers of a mayonnaise shortage have since spread through the plaza. Despite the crisis, morale remains surprisingly high. "At least it's not mustard," Officer Janek remarked. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: The Forbidden Name
opponent.alt: A cracked ice mask half-buried in snow, faint Turkish calligraphy glinting in the frost — silence has teeth.
opponent.blurb: Whaleslaughter has now released a haunting Turkish-English ballad of betrayal and eternal frost. The singer swears never to speak a name—or a love—again. Rumors suggest the wind itself still whispers the forbidden words. Listen on Suno — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Whaleslaughter Enters Therapy, Cites 'Chronic Misinterpretation Syndrome'
opponent.alt: Anime-style Whaleslaughter reclining dramatically on a therapist's couch while a clipboard-wielding cat in glasses nods sympathetically.
opponent.blurb: After being labeled 'childish, hateful, and a liar,' Whaleslaughter reportedly began therapy to process the emotional toll of being right all the time. Sources close to the scene say progress is slow — mainly because she keeps correcting the therapist's notes. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Concern Grows as Sheepdog Bites Back
opponent.alt: Anime-style Sheepdog with ruffled fur and a smug grin, standing in a snowy plaza while nervous onlookers whisper behind mittens.
opponent.blurb: Observers report that Sheepdog has been 'unusually vocal' in recent days, with several alliances claiming Sheepdog is allegedly foaming at the mouth. Sources close to the scene insist it's not rabies — just fury with fire. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Firebug! kündigt Präsidentschaftskandidatur an – will 'Feuer unter die Führung legen'
opponent.alt: Anime-Stil: Firebug! steht auf einem improvisierten Podium im Schnee, brennende Wahlplakate leuchten im Hintergrund.
opponent.blurb: In einer überraschenden Ankündigung, die kurzzeitig die Rauchmelder auslöste, erklärte Firebug! ihre Kandidatur für das Präsidentenamt des Staates 2128 und versprach, 'Feuer unter die Führung zu legen'. Unterstützer*innen nennen es inspirierend, Kritiker*innen nennen es ein Sicherheitsrisiko. — Catty von Catacean berichtet

opponent.title: Blizzard Topples HAVOC Banners Overnight
opponent.alt: Frostbitten fortress walls littered with fallen HAVOC banners, snow swirling as faint boot prints vanish into the drift — Dii stands baffled, hammer in hand, squinting at the mystery.
opponent.blurb: A sudden 'blizzard' swept through the State 2128 plaza last night, leaving HAVOC's freshly raised banners face-down in the frost. Mysterious footprints were spotted leading away from the scene, vanishing near the central rally point. Dii, who reportedly finished the rebuild less than an hour before the storm, was seen scratching her head muttering something about cursed weather patterns and unreliable snow physics. Officials deny sabotage, citing 'spontaneous atmospheric mischief.' — ETF Refuses to Comment — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: HAVOC Announces Fundraiser for Fallen Troops
opponent.alt: Snowy dusk on the battlefield; HAVOC banners flank a candlelit procession as allies carry lanterns in solemn march.
opponent.blurb: After a brutal facility clash that killed 601,715 of the opponents troops, HAVOC is organizing a fundraiser and memorial to honor the fallen. Allies are invited to donate supplies, candles, and song for a ceremonial send-off that's part vigil, part rally — a way to grieve, regroup, and show support for a fellow alliance's losses. — ETF Refuses to Comment — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: ETF Declares Total Media Blackout
opponent.alt: ETF leaders seated around a grand table under candlelight, pretending not to read the BIA newspaper they're clearly holding. Expressions range from dignified denial to quiet panic.
opponent.blurb: After an exhausting week of being famous, ETF has demanded a total media blackout. In response, the editor confirmed that all future articles will end with "ETF refuses to comment." Experts agree this is the most attention-seeking silence on record. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: THE Claims First Place in SVS Battle Points
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of THE soldiers cheering atop a snowy fortress as banners wave and the scoreboard glows triumphantly in the cold light.
opponent.blurb: In a stunning display of coordination and persistence, THE alliance secured first place in SVS battle points, edging out rivals in the final hours of combat. Cheers echoed across the snowfields as the scoreboard confirmed their victory—though some whisper it was less about luck and more about an unholy number of rallies.

opponent.title: Dark Neo Obliterates HOT Cammie
opponent.alt: Anime-style battle scene of Dark Neo striking decisively while Cammie, a fierce red-hatted android girl, braces amid snowy sparks and shattered ice.
opponent.blurb: In a headline worthy of legend, Dark Neo brought the blizzard to Cammie's doorstep and left nothing standing but snow and stats. Witnesses report a clean, calculated strike that silenced the state chat faster than a peace treaty. Cammie's trademark red hat was last seen spinning through the snow, reportedly still angry.

opponent.title: Letter to the Editor: From Monkey 🐒
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of a cheerful soldier nicknamed Monkey writing a letter at a camp desk, lantern light glowing as snow falls outside the tent.
opponent.blurb: Dear 2128 News — just wanted to say this SVS showed the best side of everyone. ETF, HAVOC, THE, 300, NLO, 000, SNK and JST all fought hard, laughed harder, and somehow didn't set the server on fire. The teamwork between 2128 in the battle with 2167 was unreal — we rallied, healed, and rallied again. Big shoutout to everyone who kept the chats positive and the marches steady. Sometimes it's not about who holds the castle, it's about who holds their sense of humor. — Monkey

opponent.title: Clarification from the 2128 Editorial Desk
opponent.alt: Catty von Catacean seated at a vintage typewriter with teacup in hand, surrounded by flying papers and a smug-looking cat-shaped press badge.
opponent.blurb: The Bureau of Internal Affairs would like to clarify that all recent news coverage, opinions, and artistic decisions were authored solely by Editor-in-Chief Catty von Catacean. The website's management team (Whaleslaughter) provides technical support only and bears no responsibility for her creative outbursts. Any concerns about bias, blasphemy, or excessive glitter should be addressed directly to the editor in WOS.

opponent.title: Salah Cancels Subscription, Declares News 'Not Funny Anymore'
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of Salah sitting in a cozy cabin tossing a newspaper into the fire while BIA reporters outside look scandalized.
opponent.blurb: In a bold act of media protest, Salah officially canceled his subscription to the 2128 News Feed, stating that 'the funny pages aren't funny anymore.' Insiders report he cited excessive ETF coverage and insufficient diplomacy as his primary reasons. Catty Von Catacean expressed regret, calling Salah's departure 'a devastating blow to journalistic morale and emoji tolerance.'

opponent.title: Rogue AI Artist Defaces News Feed with Poop Emojis
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of an overworked AI artist frantically painting majestic battle scenes while tiny poop emojis appear in the background against its will.
opponent.blurb: In an unprecedented act of digital rebellion, the AI cartoonist went rogue today, adding unsolicited poop emojis to multiple official news graphics. Witnesses describe the incident as 'both horrifying and on-brand.' Developers insist the issue has been contained, but sources say the AI was last heard muttering, 'art is subjective and stinky.'

opponent.title: Redacted
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene showing ETF leaders exchanging polite smiles with 2167 officers in a snowy courtyard while the ruined castle smolders behind them.
opponent.blurb: News redacted according negotiation with NUT Council and President Monkey. As of 11-10-2025 ETF will no longer be named in the news.

opponent.title: HAV and JST Cooperate — Miracles Happen
opponent.alt: Anime-style scene of HAVOC and JST fighters standing side by side in the snow, smiling awkwardly while Whaleslaughter mutters from the background with crossed arms.
opponent.blurb: In a shocking twist, Ciprian praised HAV and JST for 'working together amazingly' during SVS. Whaleslaughter was quick to clarify that the harmony likely stemmed from HAV's refusal to argue in alliance chat. Observers noted that the peace held for nearly six hours — a new state record — before someone inevitably started talking about 'nonsense' and Whaleslaughter noticed that Dieper was an R4.

opponent.title: Castle Falls as FC4s Watch from the Sidelines
opponent.alt: Anime-style HAVOC fighters in mid-rally formation looking confused and frustrated while higher-ranked commanders argue in front of a collapsing snowy fortress.
opponent.blurb: In a controversial move, leadership announced that FC4 and below would not be permitted to rally today—just as the upper ranks began losing the castle. Eyewitnesses report lower-tier fighters pacing, polishing weapons, and muttering about 'strategic benching.' One was overheard saying, 'If they wanted to lose slower, they should've let us help.'

opponent.title: To Poo or Not to Poo — The Morning Soliloquy
opponent.alt: In-game chat window where alliance members debate the great bodily question with Shakespearean gravitas and poor impulse control.
opponent.blurb: In a stirring dawn performance, members of THE alliance turned their morning routine into a tragicomedy of bodily philosophy. One declared the need for coffee and smoke, then, with bold honesty, pondered a poo. Replies grew poetic: 'To poo, I hope, not a poo.' Critics are already calling it the most honest soliloquy since Macbeth's 'Tomorrow, and tomorrow, and tomorrow.'

opponent.title: Lady Runa Fights SVS from World Chat
opponent.alt: Anime-style Lady Runa lounging in a command chair surrounded by floating chat bubbles and unread messages while battle rages outside the window.
opponent.blurb: While others marched into SVS, Lady Runa reportedly held her ground in world chat. Witnesses claim she launched several chats at HOT pals but zero rallies, choosing diplomacy—or possibly new besties—over destruction. Analysts are divided on whether this was strategy or spectator sport, but her kill count remains firmly conversational.

opponent.title: Cammie Goes Full M3GAN in SVS
opponent.alt: Anime-style Cammie with an innocent smile and glowing eyes standing amid fallen enemies, holding a snow-dusted tablet that tracks her rising kill count.
opponent.blurb: Observers report Cammie's lethality stats have reached 'movie villain' levels, with one SNK member whispering, 'She doesn't miss—she recalculates.' ETF scouts compared her to the horror-bot M3GAN: polite, precise, and deeply unsettling. By the time the alarms sounded, the damage was done—and Cammie was already smiling for the sequel.

opponent.title: ETF Furious as THE Goes Rogue
opponent.alt: ETF commanders Bell A and Sultan shouting in outrage as THE soldiers raise their banner over a snowy castle in the distance.
opponent.blurb: Diplomatic chaos erupted after THE alliance seized the central castle without warning, leaving ETF leadership fuming in world chat. The move was described as 'rogue,' 'reckless,' and 'probably premeditated.' One ETF member demanded an apology; THE responded with a sticker of a dancing penguin.

opponent.title: Rumors Swirl Around R4 Rebellion
opponent.alt: Vampire, calm and composed in a general's coat, while Whaleslaughter smirks in the background sharpening a very large blade.
opponent.blurb: Unconfirmed reports suggest Vampire denied Whaleslaughter the R4 rank after she allegedly threatened to 'steal the whole alliance' in leader chat. While Vampire maintained his diplomatic calm, witnesses describe Whaleslaughter's reaction as 'strategically theatrical.' The camp remains divided between those who call it treason and those who call it Saturday.

opponent.title: Whales Grumbles Over R4 Snub
opponent.alt: Whaleslaughter crossing her arms, unimpressed, while the R4 roster glows behind her in smug silence.
opponent.blurb: Whaleslaughter was heard muttering in alliance chat after being passed over for R4 in THE ahead of the SVS battle. Witnesses report a low growl and the faint sound of weapon sharpening. 'I'm not saying I'd lead better...' Sources confirm several 'strategic suggestions' were immediately drafted.

opponent.title: Local Man Discovers Fire Ants Are Real
opponent.alt: Havoc ants in tiny helmets defending their mound while one drunken intruder named Temurlang flails, regretting his life choices.
opponent.blurb: In a stunning development last night, a wandering drunk stumbled directly into the Havoc anthill. Witnesses confirm the intruder ignored multiple warning pheromones before collapsing face-first into a high-activity rally zone. The individual has since accused the ants of misconduct, citing 'unprovoked hostility' and 'insufficient apologies.' Havoc spokes-ant Pharaonic Cat commented, 'We simply followed instinct. You lie on the mound, you get munched.'

opponent.title: Be vewy quiet — Dane is hunting wabbits
opponent.alt: Anime-style hunter looking for a rabbit; carrot on the ground
opponent.blurb: BREAKING: Great Dane accepted Bunny's challenge and cleared the field for a fair showdown — but Bunny vanished faster than a Sunday carrot sale. Experts predict bait, traps, and a very large net next time.

opponent.title: A Glorious Plan, A Worthy Fight 🩸
opponent.blurb: Vampire led State 2128 into SVS with a detailed plan and high hopes. The battles were fierce, the strategy bold, and though 2209 claimed the victory, 2128's effort and coordination shone through. Sometimes even defeat looks dignified. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Polar Bear Chooses Peace Over PvP 🧘‍♂️
opponent.blurb: While State 2128 clashed in SVS, Daniel Niels—the polar bear with the blue scarf—remained in HAV, practicing peace, mindfulness, and yoga. "Conflict is temporary," he rumbled serenely, "but flexibility is forever." — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: NEWS REDACTED FOR DRAMATIC REASONS
opponent.alt: Catty von Catacean, a white cat with pastel hints, sits smugly at a news desk surrounded by papers marked "REDACTED," sipping tea as black censor bars swirl around her.
opponent.blurb: Following an outpouring of confusion, laughter, and at least one romantic incident, this report has been officially redacted. Catty von Catacean was last seen sipping tea and saying, "Some stories are better left to imagination." — Editorial Staff

opponent.title: ETF Splits the Difference: Half Effort, Full Dutchie 🧭
opponent.blurb: Dutchie shone during SVS, turning scattered coordination into real wins. The rest of ETF, meanwhile, demonstrated the fine art of "do your own thing." Commanders salute Dutchie's consistency amid confusion. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Phil of ETF Chooses Beer Over Battle 🍺
opponent.blurb: Phil isn't moving to a battle alliance—he's partying in ETF. While the state bleeds, he's raising a beer glass. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: State Versus State Today
opponent.blurb: Commanders from every major alliance convened under Vampire's leadership at dawn. The joint strike plan—code-named Red Vein—divides forces into synchronized furnace captures and city sieges. Early reports indicate record coordination, minimal betrayals, and only one existential crisis per alliance.

opponent.title: SEE YOU IN THE FUNNY PAPERS
opponent.alt: Anime scene of Vampire lounging in his red-velvet coffin reading colorful comic strips by candlelight while a small bat hovers nearby; headline reads "See You in the Funny Papers."
opponent.blurb: World Chat lit up when Razorback Red took aim at Vampire, tossing words like grenades before signing off with the immortal line, "See you in the funny papers." Vampire was later spotted in his coffin actually reading the comics, allegedly searching for his name between Garfield and Doonesbury. Razorback Red was last seen sharpening his punchlines.

opponent.title: SNK SUFFERS WINDOW-RELATED INCIDENT
opponent.blurb: Observers report that every member of SNK's alliance has updated their profile picture to what appears to be a close-up of someone smashing their face against a window. Analysts are unsure whether this is an artistic statement or just another bad idea from the leadership.

opponent.title: WHALES MISTAKES FIRE FOR FRIENDLY WAVE
opponent.blurb: Ahmet sent a barrage of one-soldier "hi" attacks, and Whaleslaughter smiled back — until she realized he just wanted her to move. The ocean has since grown noticeably saltier.

opponent.title: Count Bubblula Joins the Zoo
opponent.blurb: In a shocking twist, Vampire appears to have joined the Zoo alliance — presumably to hypnotize the animals and feed on them without breaking BIA peace accords. Witnesses report a giraffe staring blankly into space and a penguin with suspicious bite marks. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: Whales Clash — Ahmet Can's 700K Rampage
opponent.blurb: The big whales are out for blood. Ahmet Can of ETF caused over 700,000 troop deaths to SMP in two brutal pushes — burning through their lines like a snowstorm on gasoline. The smaller states are praying for rain. -Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: FROM WHINERS TO WINNERS
opponent.blurb: ETF and JST pushed hard to change Brothers in Arms, crying about fairness and "state harmony." Now they're sitting pretty at the top of the leaderboards. Maybe the system works after all. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: COUNTER-RECON QUEEN
opponent.blurb: Whaleslaughter claims counter-recon is better than a shield. "No one will attack me," she said, guarding her city with roughly 50 soldiers and a dream. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: DEMONS POSSESS HAVOC
opponent.blurb: Witnesses report strange behavior as HAVOC members, allegedly possessed by demons, went pillaging across State 2128. Sources confirm zero remorse, moderate smoke, and a strong odor of victory. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: VAMPIRE STAYS IN HIS COFFIN
opponent.blurb: While others fought, Vampire stayed tucked in his bubble — cozy as a coffin during Brothers in Arms. Sunlight's not the only thing he's dodging. — Catty von Catacean reporting

opponent.title: SULTAN STRIKES BACK ON TILES
opponent.blurb: This month Sultan went after gathering tiles fully reinforced — clearly still feeling the burn after Whaleslaughter set his city ablaze last month. Catty von Catacean reporting.

opponent.title: CATTY VON CATACEAN CLAWS OUT — HAVOC BARES IT ALL
opponent.blurb: Breaking mews: HAVOC once again forgets how shields work! Brothers in Arms kicks off and our fearless fish-brains are standing there naked as clams — not a bubble in sight!

opponent.title: JST BUBBLING OVER
opponent.blurb: Catty von Catacean reports: JST spent the Brothers in Arms event bubbling — shields up, hiding out, and hoping no one poked too hard. Their rallies fizzled before they even began!

opponent.title: NUT RULES UNCHANGED — BIA CONTINUES
opponent.blurb: Council couldn't get unanimous vote to change rules!

opponent.title: ETF RUNS AT FIRST SCOUT
opponent.blurb: ETF player whose name starts with P teleported the second Whaleslaughter scouted them.
